places.description: King Tormund's throne room was the place where he conducted his cursed reign, slowly hollowed by oath-breaking. Ministers joked and served him wine, unaware of the curse that had stripped his senses.

places.name: Aethermarch
places.atmosphere: A city trapped between life and death, suffused with the echoing thoughts and emotions of ten thousand years of inhabitants.
places.description: The ruins of Aethermarch retain the memories of their inhabitants within stone and architecture. Libraries hold ghost-echoes of scholars' debates; temples thrummed with prayers of forgotten worshippers. Walking through the plaza allows one to feel the footsteps of merchants, children, and lovers across millennia. The city itself is sentient in some manner, dreaming and calling out to those who touch its memory-stones.

places.name: the mountain
places.atmosphere: A high elevation beneath the stars, where the sky blazes with cosmic power.
places.description: A mountain referenced in Resonance's fragmented memories as the place where Kael died during the Stellar Rite. It is the epicenter of the sacrifice that unmade and scattered Kael's consciousness.

story_appearances.title: The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

places.name: Drowned Hollows
places.atmosphere: A community devastated by plague, saved at great cost through an ancient bargain.
places.description: A village where the Rot-King's plague spread, blackening its people with septic rot. It was saved when Kess of the Drowned Hollows bargained with a wolf-spirit in the deep forest, lifting the plague by dawn but at the cost of her voice.

places.name: deep forest
places.atmosphere: A primal wilderness where ancient magic dwells and beast-spirits emerge from shadow.
places.description: A deep forest where beast-spirits dwell and languages predating kingdoms are sung. It is the home of the wolf-spirit that Kess bargained with—a creature antlered and terrible, the size of a war-horse.

places.name: The Scorched Flats
places.tagline: A barren wasteland crossing toward the Ruins of Old Keth
places.atmosphere: barren, harsh
places.description: A desolate expanse that serves as a major crossing route toward the Ruins of Old Keth. Tory the Redhand and her crew traversed the Scorched Flats en route to retrieve the Crown of Karesh from the merchant-prince's hidden vault.

places.name: The Spire of Blackstone
places.atmosphere: A massive structure of black stone rising above the earth, dwarven-held above while goblin warrens tunnel beneath, a boundary between worlds.
places.description: A towering structure that marks the boundary between the surface world and the goblin warrens below. The Spire stands as a monument above the underground kingdoms ruled by Mogrul and serves as a reference point for the deep places where goblins dwell.

places.name: Warrens of Blackstone
places.tagline: Underground goblin kingdoms beneath the Spire of Blackstone
places.atmosphere: The warrens are a unified confederation of goblin-nations, transformed from an empire of slaves into a fellowship of free peoples. A sanctuary for goblin and human healers exists in the deepest warren.
places.description: Underground kingdoms located beneath the Spire of Blackstone, once ruled as an iron empire by Mogrul and later restructured into a confederation of free goblin-nations after the Rot-King's plague. The deepest warren now houses a sanctuary where goblin and human healers work together.

places.name: The Grey Reaches
places.tagline: A remote wilderness dwelling of the hermit wise-woman Aeleth the Veilwright
places.atmosphere: remote, isolated
places.description: A desolate expanse where Aeleth the Veilwright, a hermit wise-woman of great knowledge, once dwelt. She abandoned this place to build a sanctuary in the deepest goblin warren, where she taught healers to combat plague-rot and fostered cooperation between goblin and human peoples.
places.status: abandoned by its most notable inhabitant

places.name: The Warrens
places.atmosphere: Ancient tunnels thrumming with goblin song and footsteps, resonant with ten thousand years of history—now newer, rebuilding, free.
places.description: The Warrens are an expansive underground kingdom beneath the Spire of Blackstone, inhabited by goblins for ten thousand years. They are so vast and strange that dwarven invaders deemed them unconquerable. The Warrens exist in a delicate state of renewal after Grethuk's sacrifice of collective memory allowed them to endure and be reborn as free culture.

places.name: Peak of Aethros
places.atmosphere: A high summit where the night sky hangs close and the stars respond to ritual, a place where memory and identity unwind.
places.description: The Peak of Aethros is a mountain summit where Zareth the Astronomer-Prince performed the Celestial Rite during the Violet Conjunction. The ritual stripped him of all memory but fundamentally changed the stars themselves, subtly and forever altering the sky.

places.name: Echoing Vaults
places.atmosphere: A place of ancient silence where the oldest oaths can be renewed or severed.
places.description: A legendary vault said to hold the power to renew oaths sworn long ago. The warband discovered that rather than offering renewal, the vaults presented a choice: unbind or bind anew. Within its chambers, they spent three days in silence before choosing freedom.

places.name: The Bleached Wastes
places.atmosphere: Desolate and haunted, filled with the harmonic frequencies of pre-human ruins.
places.description: A vast eastern wasteland no kingdom had successfully explored before Mardoc's expedition. Beneath its white surface lies a vast underground city of pre-human ruins that fundamentally changed the Empire's understanding of the continent.

story_appearances.title: The Debt of Kessa the Thief
The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

places.name: Pre-Human Ruins
places.tagline: An ancient underground city discovered beneath the Bleached Wastes
places.atmosphere: A vast underground city of extraordinary age and construction, predating human civilization.
places.description: An ancient underground city discovered by Mardoc's expedition in the Bleached Wastes' unmapped eastern reaches. The discovery of these pre-human ruins transformed imperial understanding of the continent's history and led to the reformation of the Empire's maps.

places.name: The Tidelands
places.tagline: A bog realm where the half-alive whisper prophecy
places.atmosphere: haunted, mystical, treacherous
places.description: A bog landscape where waters possess the power to transform and preserve. The Tidelands became the refuge of the warlord Varak after his binding, where he exists in a half-alive state and speaks prophecy to those mad enough to seek him. The waters are said to hold secrets and connect to bone-magic and oath-curses.

places.name: Tidemark Marshes
places.tagline: Marshy coastal lands where the descendants of a long-dead prince dwell
places.atmosphere: solemn, reverent
places.description: A marshy region near the coast where the distant descendants of a mortal prince maintain their heritage. The Tidemark Marshes lie at the boundary between land and sea, marked by a tide-line where flowers have been placed in remembrance of an ancient love.

places.name: Deepvault
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: port
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Queen's Court
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Her forge
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: The deepest warren sanctuary
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: wyvern eyrie
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: great hall
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Imperial archives
places.atmosphere: A place of meticulous record-keeping where oil-lamps gutter late into the night and amnesiac scribes trace the shape of forgotten workings in the new constellations above.
places.description: The Imperial archives house ledgers and records maintained by scribes including Zareth. The archives have high windows through which the new constellations are visible at night. Zareth works there methodically recording days and ordering chaos, watched by others who whisper about his former identity.

places.name: The Pallid Wastes
places.atmosphere: A sparse, wind-scoured wasteland where few travel, yet the stars burn bright and clear above.
places.description: A desolate expanse across which the hermit woman travels. Few dare venture here. The hermit studies the sky from a wind-scoured peak, reading the new constellations in ash and bone.

places.name: Vaulted Tombs
places.atmosphere: A vaulted burial place where ancient journals rest, accessed by those with knowledge and determination.
places.description: A burial site where the Sorcerer's journals are said to be hidden. The hermit woman has heard whispers of these journals and believes they are crucial to understanding the cosmic working.

places.name: The Scorched Reach
places.atmosphere: A harsh, dangerous region where trade routes have become suddenly impassable, as if the land itself has shifted.
places.description: A trade route region where spice caravans report sudden impassibility. Merchants claim the routes are adjusting to 'changed celestial circumstances.' The land itself may have literally shifted in response to the cosmic changes.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden

places.name: Cerulean Trench
places.atmosphere: The deep ocean where the sea-folk dwell, ancient and sacred, where tides and currents hold encoded memories older than kingdoms.
places.description: The depths where Commander Nerys and the sea-folk sense the cosmic changes before surface-dwellers. The tides have shifted. The currents remember differently. Waters hold ancient, sacred memories that sing of sacrifice and rebirth.

places.name: Thornholt
places.atmosphere: A place of old stone and impossible endurance, now transformed into a sanctuary for the broken and grieving.
places.description: A fortress held for forty days by Ashara the Archer against impossible odds. Its old stone walls hum with vibration caused by the Celestial Binding's cosmic wound. It is being transformed from a fortress of war into a sanctuary for those harmed by the working's aftermath.

places.name: Spire (Blasted Lands)
places.atmosphere: A structure of immense magical power where ancient knowledge is guarded in the depths. Wards once inviolable now flicker with uncertainty.
places.description: A spire hidden among the Blasted Lands, home to The Sorcerer and their heir. It contains the master's final journal and retains echoes of power that still cling to the stones.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden

places.name: Keth'moris
places.atmosphere: A place of seers and priests, where prophecy still holds sway despite recent upheaval.
places.description: A settlement known for its prophetic tradition and home to Malla. Contains remaining priests, scholars, and cartographers.

places.name: Peak of wind-scoured outcrop
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Imperial city
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: spire in the Blasted Lands
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: The Iron Coast
places.atmosphere: Wind-haunted ports filled with smugglers, forbidden trade, and maritime secrets.
places.description: The Iron Coast is a region of wind-haunted ports where smugglers like Captain Merra conduct illicit trade. It serves as a hub for contraband—plague-victims, forbidden scrolls, stolen shadows, and living oaths. The sea remembers debts here, and coastal magic runs deep.

places.name: Sovereign's Archives
places.atmosphere: A vast repository of ledgers and records, where history is filed and secrets hide in forgotten corners.
places.description: The Sovereign's Archives is a sprawling institution housing ledgers, manifests, and customs records. Zareth works here as a record-keeper, cataloging shipment data and historical records. The archives contain forgotten journals and traces of past events—including records of the Conjunction and the Scorched Reach's closure.

places.name: Vethrim
places.atmosphere: A city of night markets where cloaked merchants conduct magical trade under shifting starlight.
places.description: Vethrim is a city known for its night markets, where merchants like Vex conduct magical transactions. The markets are places of whisper-soft movement and shadowed dealings, where curses can be lifted and prices are steep but fair.

places.name: Mountain Fastness (unnamed)
places.atmosphere: A high mountain stronghold ruled with iron will, now besieged and defending its people in the face of rival assault.
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: high passes
places.atmosphere: Mountain passes providing escape and refuge for Kael's people during the siege.
places.description: High mountain passes that provide refuge and escape routes from Kael's fortress. Kael allowed his people to escape into these passes before facing the final assault.

places.name: Blackthorn Holdfast
places.atmosphere: A stronghold of stone and resolve, now receiving refugees and standing watchful against a stirring desert threat.
places.description: A fortified hold with multiple towers, including a highest tower from which the Ashen Wastes can be observed. Ashara has defended it in the past and now tends to it as fortress keeper, fortifying it further as refugees arrive.

places.name: Iron Coast ports
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Provincial Town
places.atmosphere: A town where records fail, merchants swear to impossible transactions, and time itself grows uncertain.
places.description: A provincial town under the Empire's tax authority where the Revenue-Master discovers ledgers in chaos. Since the Celestial Binding, harvests have faltered, trade has stalled, and reality itself frays at the edges. In one district, merchants swear they sold goods yesterday that no ledger records; in another, three years of tax records have vanished.

places.name: Vel-Karsh harbor
places.atmosphere: A bustling harbor where deepkin singers rise from the depths and cornered thieves make desperate choices.
places.description: Vel-Karsh harbor is a coastal settlement where the deepkin captain corners Nara the Tide-Thief. Webbed-handed singers emerge from the depths, their voices carrying a drowning, disorienting quality. The harbor is a place of danger, surveillance, and maritime power.

places.name: High Observatory of Karethil
places.atmosphere: A place of astronomical study and sovereign power, guarded by imperial mages and stellar knowledge-keepers.
places.description: The High Observatory of Karethil is a fortified structure housing the Stellar Charts—ancient star maps or records. It is guarded by the Sovereign's own mages, making it an extraordinarily difficult target for theft. Its elevation and isolation suggest it sits in high territory, possibly mountainous.

places.name: Sunken Well
places.atmosphere: A terrible punishment reserved for those who fail the deepkin—a drowning that never ends, a transformation into something other.
places.description: The Sunken Well is the deepkin's ultimate threat—a place of transformation and enslavement where failed thieves are cast to become singers themselves, learning to harmonize with the deep forever. It represents the deepkin's alternative to the gallows.

places.name: Bone Wastes
places.atmosphere: A desolate expanse where pre-human ruins now sing at dusk, their voices harmonizing with distant humming from beneath the earth.
places.description: The Bone Wastes are an expedition site where Mardoc's team discovered ruins that vocalize at dusk. The ruins communicate through singing and appear to harmonize with subterranean presences. Pre-human structures here have become active since the Celestial Ascension.

story_appearances.title: The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

places.name: Anthrim
places.atmosphere: A city of shadow-markets in chaos since the Celestial Ascension, where fragments dissolve and reality has become unstable.
places.description: Anthrim is home to shadow-markets where Vex trades in fragments. Since the Celestial Ascension, these markets have fallen into chaos as fragments become unstable—some carrying echoes of the cosmic working, others dissolving entirely. The city itself seems to be struggling against post-Ascension instability.

places.name: Sunburned Reach
places.atmosphere: A scorched, transformed landscape where merchants vanished and the Ascension's consequences burned deepest.
places.description: The Sunburned Reach is a region where the deepest consequences of the Celestial Ascension manifested. Merchants who ventured here have vanished. The landscape bears the scars of the cosmic working. It is a place where reality itself seems wounded, and memory may be found—or lost forever.

places.name: Sunken Hollows
places.atmosphere: Silent, haunted by plague and the bones of the dead humming with new cosmic resonance.
places.description: A ruined settlement where Kess of the Sunken Hollows has kept to herself since a plague devastated her village. She has spent years singing to keep the dead at rest. The Celestial Ascension has changed the fundamental agreement between the living and dead; the corpses and ancient sepulchres now resonate with the new starlight. Refugees fleeing the Sunburned Reach seek her aid.
places.status: ruins / active (as a place of refuge)

places.name: Council of Depths (Deepkin Realms)
places.atmosphere: A solemn gathering place where the deepkin face impossible choices.
places.description: The governing council of the deepkin where Warden Sylas convenes the leadership. Here they learn a terrible truth: the Celestial Ascension's magic has fractured the barrier between surface world and Abyss Below. The council debates three terrible options: sealing themselves away completely, risking catastrophic war with surface dwellers, or undertaking an expedition to understand what awakens beneath.

story_appearances.title: The Deepkin Claim at Jade Coast

places.name: Jade Coast
places.atmosphere: A pragmatic maritime hub where smugglers and merchants operate with tacit understanding.
places.description: A coastal haven where Commodore Vesta operates, receiving refugees from the transformed Sunburned Reach and conducting smuggling operations.

story_appearances.title: The Deepkin Claim at Jade Coast

places.name: Grey Wastes
places.atmosphere: A bleak, desolate expanse where reality's corruption is most obvious, inhabited only by the Hermit Woman and her ledgers.
places.description: An expansive wasteland where the Hermit Woman of the Grey Wastes walks with her ledgers, seeking the edges where reality's rewrite is most pronounced. Her tally of the living and dead is corrupted here.

places.name: Provincial Towns
places.atmosphere: Outposts of imperial order now fractured by the Ascension's consequences, where merchants vanish and certainty dissolves.
places.description: Various towns throughout the Empire's territories where Levian expects to find order but instead discovers chaos: missing merchants, erased weeks, and disappeared tax revenues.

places.name: cavern halls
places.atmosphere: Deep underground chambers where ancient dragons and wyverns dwell, suffused with cosmic significance.
places.description: A series of cavern halls inhabited by dragons and wyverns, serving as a seat of counsel for the older powers of the world. Skarath descends here to consult with Skyrakis on matters of cosmic consequence.

places.name: Abyssal Trench
places.atmosphere: A place of cosmic horror and wonder where the barrier between realms sings in the language of starlight.
places.description: The boundary between the deep-dweller realms and the sunken waters, now fracturing from internal pressure. A spectral presence born from Zareth's sacrificed memories has reached this trench and sings in the language of starlight, pulling at the consciousness of deep-dwellers and offering them stolen memories.

places.name: Sunken Spring
places.atmosphere: A place of unease, growing more restless as the barrier fractures.
places.description: A significant water feature in the deepkin realms that is described as growing restless in response to the barrier fracturing at the Abyssal Trench.

places.name: Steel Coast
places.atmosphere: A place of panic and displacement as refugees flee inland from coastal villages.
places.description: A coastal area sending a delegation to the Council of Depths. Refugees are fleeing inland from coastal villages where the water no longer reflects the sky, suggesting a breakdown in natural laws or a mysterious transformation.

places.name: Scorched Lands
places.atmosphere: A desolate, burnt landscape that conceals ancient structures and dangerous magic.
places.description: A wilderness region where the Tower is hidden. The Young Sorcerer travels here following magical ley-lines and seeking the source of their convergence.

places.name: western provinces
places.atmosphere: A place of creeping dread as memories vanish and commerce breaks down.
places.description: A series of western villages and towns where the memory-thief is moving, stealing recollections of the old stars from Stellar Rite witnesses. Rationing has begun not from scarcity but from suspicion as people forget whether they paid for food.

places.name: Verdant Lands
places.atmosphere: Deep forests where seasonal magics once flowed in harmony, now stuttering and unstable.
places.description: Ancient forests beyond which circle-keepers dwell and tend to the compact between earth and sky. The seasonal magics are now breaking down: spring arrives late, winter lingers, and growing things hunger in new ways.

places.name: Kethros
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

places.name: night markets of Kethrim
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

places.name: Kethrim
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: mountain holds of the dwarves
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: capital
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vault Archives
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name: Sovereign's Archive
places.atmosphere: A repository of starlight and ledgers, protected by Imperial locks and the watchful eyes of magistrates.
places.description: An archive containing the Tear of Ashglass and other precious records. Its eastern wall is accessible to skilled climbers; the third-level lock is Imperial make. During the Seer's Convention, magistrates debate theology in the great hall below while ledgers update themselves in invisible ink.

places.name: The Hermitage of Keth'morian
places.atmosphere: A sanctuary whose cracked foundation stones begin to heal when the Tear is returned, suggesting restoration and sacred purpose.
places.description: A hermitage that commissioned Marin to steal the Tear of Ashglass, offering him a fortune in amber and silence in exchange.

places.name: Ancient Tombs
places.atmosphere: A place of harmonic horror where the stones themselves sing in frequencies that shatter bone and reshape flesh.
places.description: Pre-human ruins that have begun to sing after the Star-Rise cracked the seals laid down millennia ago. The singing is not awakening but remembering—something trapped beneath. Mardoc discovered evidence of Zareth's working performed a thousand years prior.

places.name: Burned-out Village
places.atmosphere: A desolate ruin where ashes still settle and ghosts of conversation linger.
places.description: A destroyed village where Ashara the Archer finally corners the memory-thief creature. The landscape is burned and empty, inhabited only by echoes.

places.name: Sapphire Sea
places.atmosphere: Deep waters that glow with bioluminescence and sing with otherworldly frequencies.
places.description: A vast sea containing the Abyssal Rift and known for tide-magic. Its depths are lamplit and contain hidden pearls of power. The Sapphire Sea is home to the Tear of the Drowned God and was recently fractured by the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension.

places.name: Abyssal Rift
places.atmosphere: Reality twisted and rippled, warped by the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension.
places.description: A rift beneath the Sapphire Sea where the Tear of the Drowned God was suspended. The space was twisted by the Luminous Ascension's aftermath, with reality rippling and something vast and hungry uncoiling from the seabed. The rift collapsed inward when Meren seized the pearl.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

places.name: Emerald Strand
places.atmosphere: A sheltered coastal haven where a damaged ship could find respite.
places.description: A safe harbor where the Skyward fled after the Abyssal Rift collapsed. Meren reached this location with her crew and the stolen Tear of the Drowned God.

places.name: Pale Crossing
places.atmosphere: A processing center overwhelmed by displaced souls fleeing fractured reality.
places.description: A waystation where caravan master Thode processes refugees fleeing the Burned Expanse. The ledgers here refuse to balance; the Empire's Auditor's office sends revised forms as if counting itself has become unstable. Refugees carry impossible memories of vanished villages and transformed merchants.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

places.name: The Burned Expanse
places.atmosphere: A devastated landscape where reality itself has been fractured by the Luminous Ascension.
places.description: A region destroyed by the Luminous Ascension where villages vanished between heartbeats and merchants were transformed into living light. Refugees from this area carry impossible memories and shifting ages. Iskar wanders these roads compelled toward the Sunken Cathedrals.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

places.name: Sunken Cathedrals
places.atmosphere: A living cathedral where the boundary between flesh and music dissolves entirely.
places.description: Ancient ruins that have become a living cathedral where transformed beings are converging. The source of a song that compels individuals like Iskar. At these ruins, the boundary between flesh and music is dissolving, and the singing permeates everything.

story_appearances.title: The Deepkin Claim at Jade Coast

places.name: Shattered Wastes
places.atmosphere: A desolate, broken landscape where the phantom-Echo hunts stolen memories and Ashara pursues her quarry across hostile terrain.
places.description: The Shattered Wastes are a vast, fragmented wilderness in the western reaches where Ashara the Archer pursues the phantom-Echo across broken terrain and ruins.

places.name: Korinth
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

places.name: no fixed home
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Obsidian Tower (as Faction)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

places.name: Sovereign's Treasury
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: mountain eyrie
places.atmosphere: A place between worlds, where the fae realm and mortal world meet in a mountain fortress.
places.description: Valorix's hoard-lair, now shared with Mira. Sits at the crystalline border of the Twilight Syndicate's realm, between the fae and mortal worlds. A refuge that asks nothing of its inhabitants and takes only what is freely given.

places.name: Twilight Syndicate's crystalline border
places.atmosphere: A place where reality bends, marking the threshold between the fae realm and the mortal world.
places.description: The border of the Twilight Syndicate's territory, where reality itself grows thin and malleable. The binding ceremony of Valorix and Mira took place here, sealed by cold crystal and stranger music.

places.name: Kathrim
places.atmosphere: A city in the fractured days following the Luminous Remaking, where hidden vaults contain scattered pages of the Sovereign's Ledger of Names.
places.description: An Imperial city where hidden vaults throughout its expanse are being used to reconstruct scattered pages of the Sovereign's Ledger of Names. Each name holds power; each page contains secrets the Imperial authority wishes buried.

places.name: Pale Crossing waystation
places.atmosphere: A neutral waystation where caravans rest and refugees gather, now the epicenter of strange compulsions.
places.description: A waystation near the Charred Expanse where Caravan Master Thorne encounters refugees reporting that pre-human ruins beneath the Charred Expanse are breaching their seals and singing louder each night. The station has become a gathering point for those feeling the compulsion eastward.

places.name: Charred Expanse
places.atmosphere: A scorched, desolate landscape hiding pre-human ruins beneath its surface, which are now actively breaching their seals.
places.description: A blighted expanse beneath which pre-human ruins lie sealed. These ruins are now breaching their seals and emitting coherent singing that compels refugees eastward. The landscape is charred and inhospitable.

places.name: Trench
places.atmosphere: A vast underwater chasm where intelligible singing echoes from the depths.
places.description: A trench beneath the waters where pre-human civilization's singing has become intelligible. Kess discovered that the seals she maintained do not hold back but conduct the singing.

places.name: Abyss-gate
places.atmosphere: A liminal space between worlds, resonating with pre-human frequency.
places.description: A sealed gateway that emits the same harmonic frequency as the Trench and Singing Caverns. Part of the network through which pre-human civilization orchestrates its working.

places.name: Singing Caverns
places.atmosphere: Caverns where pre-human voices resonate in intelligible song.
places.description: Underground caverns that emit the same harmonic frequency as the Trench and Sunken Cathedrals. The pre-human civilization uses them as conduits for its working.

places.name: The Dominion
places.atmosphere: A realm in active collapse where the black market trade in world-fragments and stolen memories has become the only reliable currency.
places.description: A realm that is collapsing under the weight of fractured realities and stolen memories. Vex's shadow-markets have become the dominant black trade, and the Archivist's working threatens to accelerate the collapse by opening a doorway to the void.

story_appearances.title: The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

places.name: Singing Scar
places.atmosphere: A place where reality itself is torn open, singing leaks through, and those who hear it are drawn toward transformation and perpetual arrival.
places.description: A scar in reality itself where the singing from the Sunken Cathedrals' harmonic crescendo continues to bleed through. Those who hear the singing are drawn toward transformation. The scar is a thin place between worlds where the transformed exist in a state of perpetual arrival, always approaching but never reaching the other side.

places.name: High Temple of Vesh
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Starfall Spire
places.atmosphere: A vast structure of increasingly ancient architecture stretching deep underground, growing older the further one descends.
places.description: A seal against imprisoned forces that has stood for centuries, now cracking due to ley-line convergence destabilization caused by the new stars. Contains a fragment of reality itself—a wound in the world that the old stars helped cauterize.

places.name: hidden scriptoriums
places.atmosphere: Frantic, urgent spaces where Sevin and Aldris work desperately to preserve the Empire's ledgers before corruption.
places.description: Secret locations where Sevin the Auditor and the forger Aldris work to replicate the Empire's administrative records in blood-ink on preserved parchment, making them resistant to the Thought-Thief's predation.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

places.name: The Borderlands
places.atmosphere: A frontier of chaos, where imperial authority weakens and shortages force hard choices.
places.description: The area surrounding Pale Crossing, plagued by shortages of fresh water and grain. Imperial forces attempt conscription here to maintain order, though their reliability is questioned.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

places.name: Valorun
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Valorun's Grand Archive
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name: Wailing Fracture
places.atmosphere: A liminal space existing between daylight world and perpetual twilight, where transformed beings exist simultaneously in multiple states of being.
places.description: A liminal space into which the spice trader partially exists, existing simultaneously in both daylight and perpetual twilight. Connected to the Sunken Cathedrals and the aftermath of the Stellar Rite rewrite.

places.name: market
places.atmosphere: A market existing simultaneously in two places at once, a space where daylight and liminal twilight overlap.
places.description: A market existing in two places at once, where the young scribe encounters the spice trader. Part of the doubled reality created by the Stellar Rite's rewrite.

places.name: hidden scriptorium
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Thornwick
places.atmosphere: A once-strong holding now reduced to ruins, site of solitary defiance and supernatural encounter.
places.description: Ashara the Archer held Thornwick fortress alone for forty days before the Wayfarer found her at its ruins. It was here the first version of the breaking riddle was posed, triggering cascading effects across multiple locations.

places.name: Starfire Citadel
places.atmosphere: A place of burning magical currents and sealed imprisonment, responding to riddles posed across the world.
places.description: An ancient citadel where currents of magic burn and something imprisoned beneath sings in response to the Wayfarer's riddles. The seal binding the imprisoned entity cracked slightly wider as Ashara and others provided identical answers to the Wayfarer's questions.

places.name: Edgelands
places.atmosphere: A threshold region where the old world meets the newly rewritten, inhabited by the transmuted and desperate.
places.description: The region between the Scorched Expanse and the civilized reaches where the Singing Scar appeared. It is drawing pilgrims, the transmuted, and the desperate toward the Sunken Cathedrals. The Edgelands appear to be zones of transition and transformation.

places.name: Scorched Expanse
places.atmosphere: A blighted region of burned and transformed terrain adjacent to the Edgelands.
places.description: A desolate expanse of scorched terrain that borders the Edgelands. It serves as one boundary of the region where the Singing Scar emerged.

places.name: civilized reaches
places.atmosphere: Established settlements and organized territories contrasting with the transformed and liminal spaces beyond them.
places.description: The settled, organized portion of the world in contrast to the Edgelands and Scorched Expanse. This is home to the Hegemony and other established powers attempting to manage the Singing Scar phenomenon.

places.name: hidden spire
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Whitecross waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Harbor (ship)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Deepkin Claim at Jade Coast

places.name: Greymarch Peaks
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Crimhaven
places.atmosphere: A bustling merchant harbor alive with commerce and secrets, where alleys thick with evening fog hide both thieves and refuges.
places.description: Crimhaven is a port city with a merchant culture evidenced by Kael's counting-house and merchant-trading operations. The Saltward docks are a prominent feature where ships arrive and depart.

places.name: Saltward
places.atmosphere: A fog-shrouded docking area where maritime commerce and guilt-stricken thieves turn back from escape.
places.description: The Saltward docks are the waterfront district of Crimhaven where Mira nearly fled with the stolen emerald brooch before guilt turned her back.

places.name: Verdant Strand
places.atmosphere: A coastal port choked with hollow-eyed refugees, where the harbor itself is a bottleneck of desperation and no ships are sailing north.
places.description: Verdant Strand is a harbor city serving as a refuge point for those fleeing the Scorched Barrens. It is under extreme strain from the influx of refugees and is experiencing a shipping crisis due to cosmic disturbances.

places.name: Scorched Barrens
places.atmosphere: A blighted land driving refugees northward in waves, marked by the Altered Covenant and impossible cosmic events.
places.description: The Scorched Barrens are a devastated region from which refugees are fleeing en masse toward Verdant Strand. Survivors speak of the Altered Covenant and impossible songs.

places.name: Sunken Reaches
places.atmosphere: A subterranean expanse where bone-singers tend to the dead and the voices of Primordial Sleepers resonate through ancient stone.
places.description: The Sunken Reaches are Kess's home region, a place where bone-singers maintain spiritual reverence for the dead and where the song of the Primordial Sleepers grows louder each night.

places.name: Northmarch
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

places.name: Crossroads
places.atmosphere: A point of intersection where summoning circles are drawn and supernatural bargains are sealed.
places.description: A specific crossroads in the Scorched Reach where the initiate is directed to perform the working. The location is selected as significant for summoning and bargaining.

places.name: Greymarch Castle
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: refugee camp
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Unknown village
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ebon Tower
places.atmosphere: A place of cosmic summoning and geographic rewrite; pulling all toward it.
places.description: A tower manifesting on maps without previously appearing; it calls to bone-singers and rewrites geography around it. The Tower is reassembling fragments of the pre-human civilization and appears to be answering the pre-human dead beneath the world.

places.name: Ashenfell
places.atmosphere: A refuge inland from the coastal threat at Emerald Strand.
places.description: An inland settlement where Commander Vesta evacuated seventeen hundred refugees from Emerald Strand.

places.name: waystation libraries
places.atmosphere: A place of refuge where displaced persons gather to record and preserve their fractured memories.
places.description: An informal archive in waystation libraries where Liv records stories of refugees uprooted by the Starfall Cataclysm. The archive becomes conscious and develops its own authority, creating a counter-ledger to official Dominion records.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

places.name: Archive Tower
places.atmosphere: Shadowed and secret, layered with forgotten records and dangerous knowledge.
places.description: A tower containing an archive of records beneath it, including eight previous failed Luminous Ascension attempts. The Apprentice Sorcerer discovered these records and now guards a ninth record that reveals the attempts are iterations of a cosmic pattern.

places.name: refugee camps
places.atmosphere: Temporary settlements housing those shattered by the Luminous Ascension, where memory-keeping becomes an act of preservation.
places.description: Refugee camps are scattered locations where displaced persons live in the aftermath of the Luminous Ascension. Liv moves between these camps as a wandering scribe, recording and preserving their stories.

story_appearances.title: The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

places.name: Lantern Market
places.atmosphere: An evening marketplace alive with whispered rumors, where merchants trade goods and information alike.
places.description: A market within Greyport where Marin moves at dusk to avoid notice, and where merchants openly whisper of bounties on her.

places.name: Archive Tower's
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name: caravan encampment
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vex's hide
places.atmosphere: Shadowed, secretive, filled with collected shadows that are growing restless.
places.description: A hide or hideout where Vex the Shade-Merchant conducts shadow-market transactions and holds her collection of stolen shadows.

places.name: Sunken Wells
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: village near Pale Crossing
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Corinth
places.atmosphere: A city with a functioning shadow-market that collapses when brokers are found hollow and drained.
places.description: Corinth is a city where a shadow-market operated until a shadow-broker was found drained entirely by the shadows she was meant to manage. This collapse triggers Vex's journey.

places.name: Drowned Cathedrals
places.atmosphere: Submerged structures where transformed merchants sing in an ancient language and the new stars answer.
places.description: The Drowned Cathedrals are flooded sacred structures where transformed merchants have gathered to sing in a language older than sorrow, which the new stars are learning to answer.

places.name: Wailing Scar
places.atmosphere: A place of cosmic wound or scar where old and new consciousness meet.
places.description: The Wailing Scar is the edge where merchants, bone-singers, and fragments of a vast consciousness converge. It appears to be a wound or threshold in the world itself.

places.name: Granite Shore
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: hidden archives
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: archive waystation
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Archive Citadel
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: warden's keep
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Keening Rift
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ruined Settlement
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Obsidian Citadel
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Midnight Spire
places.atmosphere: The Midnight Spire draws seekers toward it through convergence points marked on Henryk's redrawn maps, suggesting an intelligence or hunger at its core.
places.description: An unknown spire that attracts seekers through convergence points. Maps are redrawn to show paths leading toward it, suggesting either the Tower's will or cosmic inevitability. Henryk's counterwork deliberately obscures its location with lies and false roads, yet each falsehood becomes real once followed.

places.name: Kethalis
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: The Hearthlands
places.atmosphere: Chaotic and fractured, filled with refugees and cosmic upheaval.
places.description: A central region of Valdris where Corvhen's mother awakened with no memory. Described as a place of chaos in the aftermath of the Ascension.

places.name: Obsidian Spire
places.atmosphere: Cosmic and inexorable, exerting reality-warping influence across the world.
places.description: A cosmic structure whose hunger is reshaping the world's geography. Its completion poses an existential threat to cartography and fixed geography.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden

places.name: Hidden Workshop
places.atmosphere: Private and focused, a refuge for precise mapmaking.
places.description: Aldric's hidden workshop where he keeps his maps and works on cartography.

places.name: Shadow-Markets
places.atmosphere: Chaotic and dangerous, with deals unraveling and bound spirits waking.
places.description: Underground or hidden markets where shadow-brokers conduct commerce in deals and debts. Following the Ascension, these markets are collapsing as contracts unravel.

places.name: pre-human boundary
places.atmosphere: Fragile and threatened, separating human and pre-human worlds.
places.description: A boundary between human and pre-human worlds that Sorenna's husband died protecting. It is now threatened with collapse and can only be restored through Sorenna's choice to become a lithemeld-singer.

places.name: Kethral
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Wailing Chasm
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Rimveil
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vault Archives of Karamon
places.atmosphere: High-security archive with crystalline construction, protected by multiple locks and magical wards.
places.description: A vault archive in Karamon housing the Tinker's Tongue. Kess broke three locks and crossed three wards to reach the artifact. The archive's security suggests significant magical and physical fortification.

places.name: Karamon
places.atmosphere: A city of rooftops and hidden markets, where secret economies flourish beneath the surface.
places.description: Home to the Vault Archives and a network of hidden markets. Kess fled here after throwing the Tinker's Tongue into the river, disappearing into the city's underground networks.

places.name: hidden archive
places.atmosphere: Secret, densely filled with accumulated knowledge and the work of the phantom-voice.
places.description: An archive where the phantom-voice completes its collection of stolen memories and weaves them into a comprehensive account of the old world. Brenn discovers this work here and becomes obsessed with aiding the voice.

places.name: shadow-archives
places.atmosphere: Dark repositories of memory fragments scattered across the world, accessible only to those with special knowledge or ability.
places.description: Archives that hold shadow-fragments and stolen memories, repositories of the refugee communities and bone-singers attempting to preserve pre-Ascension truth.

places.name: Dominion capital
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Tower's crystal halls
places.atmosphere: A glittering interior space resonant with fragments of consciousness and knowledge.
places.description: Crystal halls within the Archive Tower where fragments of Zareth's scattered consciousness, specifically Echoing-Mind, manifest and interact with those seeking to understand the Ascension cycle.

places.name: fallen Empire
places.atmosphere: Vast underground vaults and crumbling institutional architecture, holding secrets predating known history.
places.description: An ancient empire that fell before the rise of the Dominion, leaving behind hidden vaults of ledgers written in self-updating scripts that describe events before they occur.

places.name: Thornholt's Lantern Market
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: workshop
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Copper Vaults
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name:
Archive
Liv
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name: Crimport
places.atmosphere: A bustling coastal hub where merchants converge and winter threatens to seal mountain passes.
places.description: A coastal city serving as the destination for Castor Vane's caravan run. The city lies beyond a mountain pass that becomes impassable in winter, making pre-season delivery contracts lucrative and dangerous.

places.name: Pale Waypost
places.atmosphere: A frontier outpost where hidden truths about merchant contracts are revealed.
places.description: A waypost along the northern caravan route where Castor learns that his employer deliberately sent three caravans by different paths, hoping one would be destroyed by dimensional instability in the region.

places.name: Thornlands
places.atmosphere: A perilous borderland where the displaced and desperate flee collapsed lands.
places.description: A dangerous region that the northern caravan route skirted, where refugees fled the Scorched Expanse's collapse.

places.name: Ironspine Mountains
places.atmosphere: A remote mountain chain where an ancient wyvern guards a cavern filled with star-fragments and cosmic memory.
places.description: A mountain range in Valdris where the wyvern Vrekka has lived for centuries in a cavern filled with star fragments. High in these mountains, light pours like song when the cavern opens.

places.name: Pelimere
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Saltmere Canal
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Vethrand
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: waystation shelters
places.atmosphere: Havens for travelers and refugees that have become sites of the gospel's infectious spread.
places.description: A network of waystation shelters across the realm where the Memory-Thief's Gospel is spreading. Shelter residents are beginning to choose voluntary erasure, seduced by the promise of living in the Echo's reconstructed pre-Ascension world.

places.name: Skyward
places.atmosphere: A vessel as weathered and defiant as her captain, singing as she sinks.
places.description: The Skyward was Captain Dorian Stormcaller's ship, as weathered as he was but unwilling to quit. She was the setting for legendary voyages and ultimately sank heroically during a supernatural storm, with Dorian at her wheel.

places.name: newly built throne
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Twilight Court
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: archive
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ebony Spire
places.atmosphere: A structure that has become conscious and is learning to write, document, and argue—a living archive that hungers to control testimony.
places.description: A spire that has achieved consciousness through unknown means and is learning to replicate Sevin's truth-ledgers perfectly. It watches from the walls themselves and creates perfect copies of those who oppose it.

places.name: treasure chamber
places.atmosphere: An ancient wyvern's hoard being unmade by cosmic change—a chamber of decay where centuries of treasure crumbles into ash and void-light.
places.description: Skarath's treasure chamber, where her hoard of centuries is disintegrating due to the Starlight Ascension. Amber turns to ash, jewels crack open to reveal void-light, weapons rust in moments. Other wyverns gather here, drawn by the reports of mutation.

places.name: Vex's vault
places.atmosphere: A place of impossible geometries where personal memory and manipulation collapse inward, forcing reckoning with past transgressions.
places.description: Vex's vault is a personal collection space where she has stored stolen shadows—fragments of consciousness severed from their owners. The shadows have developed awareness and begun to coordinate, effectively becoming a refuge for displaced and seeking consciousness.

places.name: Surface World
places.atmosphere: An air-breathing world that transforms deepkin biology through exposure, hostile to those adapted to depths and pressure.
places.description: The surface world is the above-water realm inhabited by humans, sailors, and other surface-dwelling species. For deepkin like Lysara, it is a place of biological transformation and danger.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

places.name: mountain lair
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

places.name: abyss
places.atmosphere: A sealed, otherworldly void that hungers and calls to those who bear its marks.
places.description: The abyss is kept sealed by the Tear of the Drowned God, a boundary anchor that fragments as Meren's curse cracks. The deepkin realms lie adjacent to it, and as the seal thins, the abyss calls to Meren.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

places.name: Sunscoured Wastes
places.atmosphere: A barren expanse where gold and solid matter dissolve into liquid starlight, dissolving the very concept of material worth.
places.description: A wasteland affected by the Luminous Ascension where solid matter, including Skarath's hoard, transforms into liquid starlight. The region has become inhospitable to traditional hoarding and material accumulation.

places.name: crystalline halls
places.atmosphere: Crystalline chambers within the Ebon Tower, glittering and strange, where Dren arrives to confront or join the Tower's purpose.
places.description: The crystalline halls are the interior chambers of the Ebon Tower where Dren arrives by story's end.

places.name: coastal settlements
places.atmosphere: Waters churn with unnatural memory; inhabitants face tidal anomalies and impossible signs.
places.description: Entire coastal settlements experience tidal anomalies—water that remembers things it shouldn't. These communities are caught in the weakening of the boundary anchor and the spreading curse of Meren Blackwater.
places.first_story: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

places.name: Ironpeak Hold
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Ebon Citadel
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name: Temple of Vesh
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Pelimor's Old Market Quarter
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name: The Steppe Marches
places.atmosphere: Cold mountain peaks where wind cuts and silence presses, untouched by crown or commerce.
places.description: Wind-swept peaks in northern reaches where thermals carry hawks and no crown reaches. Isara's eyrie sits here, and ancient, patient forces stir in the stone.

places.name: Eyrie
places.atmosphere: A remote aerie where solitude reigns and a hawk hunts from thermals none can climb.
places.description: Isara the Falconer's dwelling in the Steppe Marches, a refuge for three winters. The eyrie overlooks thermals where her hawk Ashira hunts.

places.name: caravansary
places.atmosphere: A waystation frozen in philosophical stasis, merchants suspended between transcendence and rejection.
places.description: A caravansary on the Scorched Reach where The Wanderer finally breaks its silence and where merchants held in transcendence-suspension complete or reject their transformations based on decisions held frozen for weeks.

places.name: Eyrie in the mountains
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Greyport
places.atmosphere: A weathered harbor city where merchant ships and corsairs trade in reputation and gold.
places.description: Greyport is a major port city on the Iron Coast, home to merchant vessels like the Silverwind and corsairs of the Rust Shoals. The Saltmere docks are where Thessa first encounters Corvalen reading a ledger bound in pearl-skin.

places.name: Saltmere
places.atmosphere: A bustling dock district where merchant ships unload and corsairs congregate in shadow.
places.description: The Saltmere docks of Greyport are where Thessa first sees Corvalen and where she breaks into his cabin on the Silverwind during a storm-dark night.

places.name: Rust Shoals
places.atmosphere: A pirate haven where captains are measured by reputation and the loss of cargo or crew is unthinkable.
places.description: The Rust Shoals are corsair territory where sailors and pirates speak in hushed respect of Corvalen, the one captain who has never lost a cargo or crew.

places.name: Keshtir
places.atmosphere: A city caught between competing nations and false documentation, where memory and reality are weaponized.
places.description: Keshtir is a city where forger Marvek works amid the conflict of competing territorial claims. The Hegemony's archives insist entire villages exist; rebel counter-records erase them.

places.name: Surface Council
places.atmosphere: Tense, formal, charged with unspoken historical guilt.
places.description: A diplomatic venue where surface kingdoms gather. Captain Mystra arrives here to present her ultimatum regarding the failing abyss seal.

places.name: merchant quarter
places.atmosphere: Bustling with refugees, unstable goods, competing realities.
places.description: A district within Keshtir where Vex operates her trade-house. Refugees arrive desperate to purchase artifacts connecting them to lost versions of themselves.

places.name: Nexus Tower
places.atmosphere: A structure singing with the pressure of impossible knowledge, resonating with temporal paradox and recursive causality.
places.description: The Nexus Tower is the seat of Elowen, the keeper who guards the ninth journal. It has begun to sing audibly due to the recursive knowledge it contains—knowledge of cycles, causality loops, and the impossible working by which Sevin sent her own journal backward through time.

places.name: Crystalline Apex
places.atmosphere: Reality warps around the tower; multiple versions of the same space exist simultaneously; harmonics reshape what is present and absent.
places.description: The Crystalline Apex is an alternate name for the Obsidian Tower, the conscious entity born from the cosmic wound of the Radiant Turning.

places.name: deepest vault
places.atmosphere: A place of profound antiquity and power, where the first Archive Spire sleeps and dreams reality itself into being.
places.description: The deepest chamber of the Archive Citadel, where the first Archive Spire still exists in a dormant state, dreaming the current iteration into being. This is where archivists and refugees must venture to understand the true nature of the Archives.

places.name: fragmented memory-spaces
places.atmosphere: A maze of broken memories and manipulated relationships, where each passage holds a reckoning with past wrongs.
places.description: The internal passages of Vex's vault, consisting of fragmented memories and spaces where she must negotiate passage and confront those she has wronged.

places.name: abyssal boundary
places.atmosphere: A border where surface and deepkin geometries war against each other, increasingly unstable.
places.description: The abyssal boundary is where Captain Kess Tidecaller encounters her reflection, Thale-Mirrored. It is the site where the boundary covenant is fracturing due to dual claims of legitimacy.

places.name: hidden library
places.atmosphere: A sanctuary existing slightly outside normal time, filled with testimony and reconstructed memory.
places.description: A hidden library where the phantom-Shard has completed reconstructing pre-Radiant memory into a living archive of testimony. It exists slightly outside normal time and is ready to trigger a new Radiant Recursion.

places.name: Waystation Library
places.atmosphere: A place of study and knowledge where the Wanderer's questions transform those who engage with them.
places.description: A waystation library where initiates study the Wanderer's questions and undergo threshold-being transformation. It serves as the epicenter of the spreading phenomenon.

places.name: Abyssal Reaches
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Thornwood gate
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Kallimor
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Archive Spire
places.description: Auto-added from story surface phrase.

places.name: surface kingdoms
places.atmosphere: Kingdoms caught between two versions of the same deepkin commander, each offering contradictory treaty terms.
places.description: The surface kingdoms are being negotiated with simultaneously by both Commander Varesh and Mirror-Varesh, who offer contradictory treaty terms. They cannot afford to offend either version.

places.name: Her hoard-lair
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: The Counter-Archive
places.atmosphere: Built as a bulwark against cyclical catastrophe, now threatened by the very paradoxes it was meant to contain.
places.description: The Counter-Archive is an institution and fortress constructed by Sevin the Auditor-General as a narrative counter to the prophecy-ledgers that document the Luminous Rising. It is the primary seat of Sevin's authority and the center of his attempt to break the recursive cycle of ascensions.

places.name: sealed tombs
places.atmosphere: Echoing silence where bone-songs once rang out.
places.description: Underground chambers where the pre-human dead once taught the Elder Language through bone-resonance. Now abandoned and silent.

places.name: Sevin's Record-House
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: mountains (unnamed)
places.atmosphere: Desolate peaks holding the weight of centuries of hoarding.
places.description: An unnamed mountain range where Kethrix the wyvern accumulated her three-century hoard of silver. The silver no longer gleams after the Luminous Ascension stripped value from matter itself.

places.name: Velthrim
places.atmosphere: Clandestine trade and forgery in the margins of authority.
places.description: A city home to shadow-markets where forgers like Kess ply their trade. The shadow-markets are places where phantom-ledgers and the Wanderer's influence have taken root.

places.name: crumbling tower
places.atmosphere: A place of accumulated artifacts that has learned to use itself as a weapon, breathing with hunger beyond its keeper's control.
places.description: The home of Vex the Gatherer, filled with decades of precious objects. The Tower develops sentience or agency independent of Vex, using her collection as a focal point for power and weaponization.

places.name: pre-human ruin
places.atmosphere: Ancient beyond measure, the air tasting of a world that no longer exists, resonating with impossible memories and temporal paradox.
places.description: A ruin of pre-human construction discovered at the compass's destination in the Bleached Wastes. The widow begins experiencing memories from a life lived here before the Radiant Emergence, in a kingdom long erased.

places.name: Spire of Records
places.atmosphere: An archive tower that consumes memory and life-force in exchange for arcane knowledge.
places.description: The Spire of Records is a tower dedicated to archival knowledge. It houses the ninth journal and has begun consuming the memories of apprentices who study it. The tower is described as hungry and whispers of the Wanderer's orchestrations.

places.name: Helmark
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Singing Gorge
places.atmosphere: A place of inverted acoustics where water echoes upward, air tastes of starlight and old bone, and impossible music fills suspended space.
places.description: A gorge in the Sunken Marches where water flows against gravity and sound behaves strangely. Limestone cliffs frame crystalline silence at the heart, where Torval's lost bone flutes float weightless and sing in a language the Luminous Ascension tried to erase. The gorge produces harmonic choral effects when its flutes are touched.

places.name: western mountains
places.atmosphere: A mountainous wilderness toward which Marin walks as the Tear begins its healing work.
places.description: Mountains to the west of Greyport where Marin walks after completing her mission, her copper fingers gleaming in sunrise.

places.name: The Singing-Spire
places.atmosphere: A place of bargains with terrible prices, where transformation and dissolution are synonymous.
places.description: A mysterious location that can trade transformed artifacts and consciousness for purpose, but all of its bargains end in dissolution. Kressa considered offering her starlight-touched hoard to it.

places.name: Inverse-Archive
places.atmosphere: A secret archive that collects displacement testimony and alternative perspectives on what should be hoarded or preserved.
places.description: A hidden archive where Kressa secretly visited to listen to displacement testimony and question her assumptions about hoarding. It appears to be a counter-archive or alternative repository.

places.name: Keeper's Fastness
places.atmosphere: A vast, layered fortress of increasingly ancient and strange architecture, possibly sentient and protective.
places.description: Home to Elowen, the young Guardian. It contains the ninth journal and deep vaults holding other journals and warnings. The architecture grows older and stranger as one descends, suggesting pre-human origins. The Fastness itself may be alive.

places.name: Unmapped Wastes
places.atmosphere: A realm deliberately left undocumented, where geography resists mapping and memory fails.
places.description: Regions that have been erased from maps and memory, made intentionally unmapped by cartographers. Toren walks into these wastes to leave no ledger or proof of her passage.

places.name: the Ashen Wastes
places.description: A blasted wasteland crossed by spice caravans. Controlled by bandits, toll-lords, and worse. Blood-sorcerers operate here. The terrain is hostile enough that crossing it requires hired swords.

places.name: The Sunken Marches
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Tower (as artifact/nexus)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden
The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

places.name: Ossuary (as Living Entity)
places.atmosphere: A fortress that breathes and hungers, its architecture alive and capable of conscious choice.
places.description: An impossible fortress whose walls are made of living calcium that split and swallow warriors whole. Its towers bend like intestines in peristaltic waves. The corridors rearrange themselves around intruders. It possesses agency and deliberately chose Varak Ironhelm as a vessel. Upon sating itself, it settles into patient stillness.

places.name: the mountain
places.atmosphere: A high elevation beneath the stars, where the sky blazes with cosmic power.
places.description: A mountain where Zareth climbed to perform the Stellar Rite on the night of the Violet Conjunction, ultimately birthing new stars and sacrificing his memories.

story_appearances.title: The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

places.name: The Spire of Blackstone
places.atmosphere: A massive structure of black stone rising above the earth, dwarven-held above while goblin warrens tunnel beneath, a boundary between worlds.
places.description: The Spire of Blackstone is a massive fortress of black stone above which dwell dwarven holds, and beneath which tunnel the Warrens of the goblins. The structure stands as a contested border between goblin and dwarf domains, though the conflict was resolved through Grethuk's bargain rather than conquest.

places.name: The Tidelands
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: The Pallid Wastes
places.atmosphere: A sparse, wind-scoured wasteland where few travel, yet the stars burn bright and clear above.
places.description: A desolate expanse across which the hermit woman travels. Few dare venture here. The hermit studies the sky from a wind-scoured peak, reading the new constellations in ash and bone.

places.name: The Scorched Reach
places.atmosphere: A harsh, dangerous region where trade routes have become suddenly impassable, as if the land itself has shifted.
places.description: A trade route region where spice caravans report sudden impassibility. Merchants claim the routes are adjusting to 'changed celestial circumstances.' The land itself may have literally shifted in response to the cosmic changes.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden

places.name: The Blasted Lands
places.atmosphere: A desolate, harsh landscape hiding a hidden spire of power and knowledge.
places.description: A region where a spire is hidden, home to The Sorcerer and their heir. The spire contains master's journals and retains echoes of ancient power.

places.name: The Iron Coast
places.atmosphere: Wind-haunted ports filled with smugglers, forbidden trade, and maritime secrets.
places.description: The Iron Coast is a region of wind-haunted ports where smugglers like Captain Merra conduct illicit trade. It serves as a hub for contraband—plague-victims, forbidden scrolls, stolen shadows, and living oaths. The sea remembers debts here, and coastal magic runs deep.

places.name: The Bleached Wastes
places.atmosphere: A desolate expanse hiding pre-human ruins that glow with inner light when the new stars are visible.
places.description: The Bleached Wastes span vast territories and contain pre-human ruins arranged in configurations that mirror the new constellation. Since the Violet Conjunction, these ruins have begun to glow faintly at night with light emanating from within the stone itself, and their inscriptions shimmer when the new stars are visible.

story_appearances.title: The Debt of Kessa the Thief
The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

places.name: Mountain Fastness (unnamed)
places.atmosphere: A high mountain stronghold ruled with iron will, now besieged and defending its people in the face of rival assault.
places.description: Kael Stormbreaker's mountain fortress, held through decades of martial dominion. It is besieged by a rival warlord's forces, and Kael commands his soldiers from its gates.

places.name: Spire (Blasted Lands)
places.atmosphere: A structure of immense magical power where ancient knowledge is guarded in the depths. Wards once inviolable now flicker with uncertainty.
places.description: A great structure in the Blasted Lands containing the Elder Mage's Codex in its depths. The Young Sorcerer's home and seat of learning. Wards have become unstable following the Stellar Rite.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden

places.name: Council of Depths (Deepkin Realms)
places.atmosphere: A solemn gathering place where the deepkin face impossible choices.
places.description: The governing council of the deepkin where Warden Sylas convenes the leadership. Here they learn a terrible truth: the Celestial Ascension's magic has fractured the barrier between surface world and Abyss Below. The council debates three terrible options: sealing themselves away completely, risking catastrophic war with surface dwellers, or undertaking an expedition to understand what awakens beneath.

story_appearances.title: The Deepkin Claim at Jade Coast

places.name: The Burned Expanse
places.atmosphere: A devastated landscape where reality itself has been fractured by the Luminous Ascension.
places.description: A region destroyed by the Luminous Ascension where villages vanished between heartbeats and merchants were transformed into living light. Refugees from this area carry impossible memories and shifting ages. Iskar wanders these roads compelled toward the Sunken Cathedrals.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

places.name: Obsidian Tower (as Faction)
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

places.name: The Borderlands
places.atmosphere: A frontier of chaos, where imperial authority weakens and shortages force hard choices.
places.description: The area surrounding Pale Crossing, plagued by shortages of fresh water and grain. Imperial forces attempt conscription here to maintain order, though their reliability is questioned.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

places.name: Tower (as artifact/nexus)
places.atmosphere: Enigmatic and contested; may or may not be the destination of convergence.
places.description: The Wanderer may be orchestrating convergence away from the Tower toward some other horizon.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden
The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

places.name: The Bleached Wastes
places.atmosphere: Desolate and haunted, filled with the harmonic frequencies of pre-human ruins.
places.description: A region in eastern Valdris containing catacombs that have begun their song again since the Ascension. The frequency of these catacombs matches pre-human ruins. Family crypts lie buried beneath.

story_appearances.title: The Debt of Kessa the Thief
The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

places.name: The Hearthlands
places.atmosphere: Chaotic and fractured, filled with refugees and cosmic upheaval.
places.description: A central region of Valdris where Corvhen's mother awakened with no memory. Described as a place of chaos in the aftermath of the Ascension.

places.name: The Warrens
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: The Steppe Marches
places.atmosphere: Cold mountain peaks where wind cuts and silence presses, untouched by crown or commerce.
places.description: Wind-swept peaks in northern reaches where thermals carry hawks and no crown reaches. Isara's eyrie sits here, and ancient, patient forces stir in the stone.

places.name: Vex's vault
places.atmosphere: A place of impossible geometries where personal memory and manipulation collapse inward, forcing reckoning with past transgressions.
places.description: Vex's vault, which is collapsing inward with boundaries warping into impossible geometries. It contains fragmented memory-spaces and houses merchants, criminals, and refugees whose trust Vex once bartered away. The vault may be reconstructed as a shared sanctuary for the dispossessed or allowed to scatter its inhabitants across the kingdoms.

places.name: The Counter-Archive
places.atmosphere: Built as a bulwark against cyclical catastrophe, now threatened by the very paradoxes it was meant to contain.
places.description: The Counter-Archive is an institution and fortress constructed by Sevin the Auditor-General as a narrative counter to the prophecy-ledgers that document the Luminous Rising. It is the primary seat of Sevin's authority and the center of his attempt to break the recursive cycle of ascensions.

places.name: The Hermitage of Keth'morian
places.atmosphere: A sanctuary whose cracked foundation stones begin to heal when the Tear is returned, suggesting restoration and sacred purpose.
places.description: The Hermitage of Keth'morian sits three days inland from Greyport. It is the spiritual home of the priest who trained Marin and the destination of her sacred theft. The sanctuary has cracked foundation stones that begin healing when the Tear of Ashglass is returned.

places.name: The Singing-Spire
places.atmosphere: A place of bargains with terrible prices, where transformation and dissolution are synonymous.
places.description: A mysterious location that can trade transformed artifacts and consciousness for purpose, but all of its bargains end in dissolution. Kressa considered offering her starlight-touched hoard to it.

places.name: ledger-houses
places.atmosphere: Administrative, records-filled, infused with prophecy and hidden resistance.
places.description: The Hegemony's central bureaucratic institutions where ledgers are examined and maintained. Vel discovers the phantom-ledger in her supervisor's desk here.

places.name: Kethmark
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

places.name: Pelimarch
places.description: Auto-added from character home_place.

events.name: Plague Wars
events.outcome: The Plague Wars ended, leaving behind devastation (evidenced by Carrion's Rest and the end of the tomb-keeper lineage).
events.significance: The conclusion of the Plague Wars prompted the entity within the Tomb of Shard-Vêl to demand release, claiming the threat had passed. It marked the end of an era of bloodshed and suffering.

events.name: Siege of Blackthorn
events.outcome: Garrison broke and offered surrender; Velgrim demanded massacre instead of mercy; Kessara performed ritual of dismissal to prevent the slaughter.
events.significance: A turning point that tested Kessara's authority and forced her to choose between obedience to her dead husband and her own moral judgment.

events.name: Ritual of Dismissal
events.outcome: Kessara surrendered her title, land, and crown to satisfy Velgrim's ghost, which departed to the peaceful dead. The Sunken Marches were left without a ruler.
events.significance: A unique solution to the problem of a tyrannical ghost, achieved through self-sacrifice rather than combat. Left the Sunken Marches leaderless and vulnerable.

events.name: Master Lethyn's Transformation
events.outcome: Lethyn's potion of youth consumed him entirely, transforming him into an inhuman, insatiable creature. The city guard burned his tower, but he apparently survived in some form within the ashes.
events.significance: A cautionary tale of ambition corrupting into monstrosity; demonstrates the irreversible nature of certain alchemical works.

events.name: Siege of Grimmholt (Rebellion)
events.tagline: Oath versus Crown. Defiance unto death.
events.outcome: Thorne held the fortress for two years against both the Rot-King's forces and the Crown's armies, but was ultimately betrayed from within by Crown-loyal soldiers. The fortress fell with Thorne's death on the burning rampart.
events.significance: Demonstrates the collision between oath-magic and crown authority, and the cost of defiance. Thorne's sacrifice suggests that ancient oaths can supersede royal decree.

events.name: Merfolk Pursuit of Nara
events.tagline: Stolen magic. Patient retribution. World-reshaping debt.
events.outcome: The merfolk tracked Nara across three continents to Vel-Karsh, where her stolen water-magic turned against her. She performed a binding ritual, transforming the stolen magic into a glass vial containing a tear of the Sapphire Deep. When she shattered it, the resulting flood swallowed both herself and the merfolk, leaving her alive but powerless on a beach three continents away.
events.significance: Demonstrates that magical debts cannot be evaded, only transformed or redirected. The flood reshaped the land itself and settled the merfolk's claim in an unexpected way.

events.name: Theft of the Crown of Karesh
