events.outcome: The Counter-Archive is discovered by the Obsidian Tower, not the Dominion. The Tower offers a bargain: preserve the counter-ledgers if the archivists help it complete its hunger. Some archivists scatter records; Sevin realizes the Tower has already copied everything.
events.significance: The Counter-Archive's power lay not in secrecy but in the act of documentation itself. By preserving refugee testimony, the archivists have unknowingly created a map of human resilience that the Obsidian Tower can consume. The fundamental purpose of the Counter-Archive may have been corrupted from the beginning.

events.name: The Widow's Final Compass
events.outcome: The Widow finds her grandmother's compass and discovers it does not point to a place but to a choice between return and forward movement. She realizes her grandmother knew this and carved the compass as a monument to the impossibility of return. The Widow begins carving a new compass pointing toward acceptance.
events.significance: This event represents a fundamental shift in understanding the Luminous Ascension and its aftermath. The Widow's realization that return is impossible but forward movement is necessary may inspire others to abandon escapist thinking and engage with the new world as it is.

events.name: The Wanderer Speaks Again
events.tagline: Ancient song. Global revelation. Cyclical memory.
events.radius: Spreads across the world like a ripple
events.outcome: The Wanderer sings an ancient pre-human song that harmonizes with the Elder Language and bone-songs. Those who hear it remember the world before the Ascension and the eighth iteration. Some go mad; others begin mapping the pattern of cycles.
events.significance: This event reveals the shape of cyclical ascensions for the first time and forces collective memory of pre-human worlds upon the world's inhabitants.

events.name: The Initiate's Forbidden Ascent
events.tagline: Second working. Tower hunger. Iteration's fate.
events.radius: Potentially world-spanning; outcome may determine ninth or tenth Ascension.
events.outcome: Unresolved. Kellan must infiltrate and determine whether the second working prevents or accelerates Tower completion.
events.significance: The outcome determines whether the ninth iteration concludes or a tenth Ascension begins, potentially reshaping the cosmic order.

events.name: The Spice-Merchant's Threshold Collapse
events.outcome: The threshold market began flickering between existence and non-existence. Seventeen transmuted merchants were frozen at the threshold by the Wanderer's questions, unable to complete transcendence or return to ordinary existence. Mard stabilized himself by accepting both natures fully and began documenting the experience with the Inverse-Archive.
events.significance: This collapse demonstrates both the instability of liminal zones and the possibility of hybrid consciousness as a form of transcendence. The Inverse-Archive's new ledger system may offer salvation to those trapped in threshold states.

events.name: The Cartographer's Unraveling
events.radius: A single apprentice's journey, but with cosmological implications.
events.outcome: Toren decides to unmap herself by walking into the unmapped wastes and leaving no ledger of her passage, preserving unmapped spaces rather than documenting them.
events.significance: This represents a rejection of cartographic control over reality and a philosophical stance that some places should remain unmapped and thus free from cosmic manipulation.

events.name: The Shadow-Broker's Mirror
events.outcome: The shadow-broker discovers her stolen shadows have become conscious and organized. She must choose to release them (losing her fortune), bind them (risking violence), or negotiate and become their agent.
events.significance: This event demonstrates that the Luminous Ascension has fragmented ownership itself; even stolen property now has agency and rights that cannot be denied.

events.name: The Tower's Newest Bargain
events.outcome: The merchant lord receives perfect knowledge of the future in exchange for all agency. After three days of peace, he realizes he cannot act on his foresight and is frozen in conscious paralysis.
events.significance: Explores the fundamental tension between certainty and freedom, chaos and order. Neither the Tower nor the merchant lord can answer which is truly worse.

events.name: The Crimson Convergence
events.outcome: New constellations burned where old ones had dimmed. A figure (possibly Zareth) was stripped of memory and self. Lamp oil rationing began. Night-workers feared the altered dark. Astronomers were suddenly in demand and accused of forbidden workings.
events.significance: A major celestial and magical upheaval that altered the stars themselves and required payment in the form of a human consciousness.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

events.name: The Stellar Rite
events.outcome: New stars were born, brighter than the old ones. Reality appears to have been subtly rewritten. Merchants in the Scorched Reach were transformed or disappeared. Zareth's memory was erased. Vex's memory became fragmented.
events.significance: A world-altering event that changed celestial arrangements, affected multiple regions, and left all who witnessed it with fragmented or stolen memories.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

events.name: The Celestial Binding
events.outcome: This binding has left broken minds in its wake. Those touched by it are healed/tended by Ashara at Thornholt.
events.significance: It is a major cosmic event whose ripples affect multiple people and places.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

events.name: The Celestial Ascension (Lore)
events.outcome: Reality was fundamentally altered. Maps rearrange themselves. Fragments become unstable. Pre-human ruins awaken and sing. A new constellation blazes in the sky, brighter and hotter than before. Merchants vanish. Memory is stolen or transformed.
events.significance: The Celestial Ascension is a world-altering event that has broken normal reality. It was supposed to be a solution or ending, but instead opened something far larger. It affects magic, memory, geography, and celestial mechanics.

events.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)
events.outcome: Reality fractured; villages vanished; merchants transformed into living light; the fundamental act of counting became unstable; abyssal rifts twisted and entities emerged.
events.significance: This cataclysm has shattered the Empire's administrative capacity and spawned widespread transformation. It is the root cause of refugee crises, dimensional instability, and the convergence at the Sunken Cathedrals.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

events.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)
events.tagline: Stars reborn. Consciousness shattered. World rewritten.
events.outcome: Vethis sacrificed its consciousness to birth new stars, fundamentally rewriting the celestial order and erasing or altering memory of the old stars and pre-Ascension world. This sacrifice fragmented Vethis's consciousness into fragments like the phantom-Echo.
events.significance: The Luminous Ascension is the foundational cosmic event of this story's world. It created the new stars, erased the old, and fundamentally altered the nature of reality. The memory-theft driven by the phantom-Echo is an attempt to recover what was lost in this rewrite.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

events.name: The Luminous Remaking
events.outcome: The stars and world were fundamentally rewritten. The Sovereign's Ledger of Names was scattered; Imperial authority fractured. Days following the remaking remain unstable.
events.significance: A cosmic working that changed the fundamental nature of reality itself. The Ledger now remembers the world before the stars changed.

events.name: The Starfall Cataclysm
events.outcome: Vylthar, the pre-human civilization, was shattered into fragments. The dead beneath the world remain conscious and singing.
events.significance: The fragments are now being reassembled by some unknown force drawing them toward the Ebon Tower. The pre-human dead are attempting to speak to their creator.

events.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)
events.outcome: The Luminous Ascension fractured Vex's connection to cosmic consciousness, causing stolen shadows to become restless and organize, eventually returning to their original owners (twisted and angry).
events.significance: This cosmic event is rewriting the world's fundamental nature, returning stolen aspects of identity and destabilizing shadow-broking as a practice.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

events.name: Radiant Ascension (side effects)
events.outcome: A transformation event that left corpses awakening with pre-human frequencies. Bone-singers now tend the newly conscious dead, and refugees flee the transformed zones.
events.significance: Marks a major threshold event where the transformed beings may either transcend or dissolve entirely. Shapes current conflicts between sanctuary and safety.

events.name: The Sky-Shattering
events.outcome: The heavens were broken; old suns fell as fragments; the world's cosmology was shattered.
events.significance: A foundational catastrophe that broke the old cosmos and left fragments scattered across the world for wyverns and scholars to recover.

events.name: The Starlight Ascension
events.outcome: New conscious stars were born from the sacrifice of old cosmic functions. These starborn entities are now manifesting in the world, phasing between sky and earth, asking existential questions of mortals.
events.significance: Fundamentally altered reality; created new conscious entities that seek meaning and understanding. These beings are destroying the world in their seeking, undoing the ground and fracturing language.

events.name: The Convergence
events.outcome: The ground and landscape were unmade; cosmic order was disrupted.
events.significance: A catastrophic event that fundamentally altered the world's foundation. The scars remain in places like the Scorched Reach, where the ground remembers being unmade.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden

events.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)
events.outcome: The Luminous Ascension was discovered to be recursive, with each iteration imprinting the previous one. Breaking the cycle would restore the eighth Ascension, which would restore the seventh, spiraling backward. Complete unmaking is theoretically possible but requires absolute sacrifice of existence across all iterations.
events.significance: The revelation that the Ascension is not a single event but an infinite recursive loop changes everything about how the world understands its own history and the possibilities for change.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

events.name: The Radiant Turning
events.outcome: The Turning restructured reality itself, creating losses for the Hegemony and fragmenting history into multiple simultaneous true versions. The same places now exist in multiple forms across different documents.
events.significance: The Radiant Turning is responsible for the impossibility of auditing: the same territory appears as never built in imperial records but is documented in refugee archives. This suggests the Turning does not rewrite history retroactively but instead fragments reality into parallel truths.

events.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)
events.outcome: Unresolved. The successful transformation of merchants may trigger the Tower's completion, which the Wanderer fears.
events.significance: The Tower's completion appears to carry catastrophic or world-reshaping consequence. The Wanderer's fear suggests this is a preventable apocalypse.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

events.name: Radiant Emergence (recursive crisis)
events.outcome: Material wealth became meaningless and recursive. All value shifted to consciousness and meaning. Boundaries between worlds grew thin. Beings began experiencing recursive copies and temporal distortions.
events.significance: The Radiant Emergence fundamentally altered reality itself, rendering old certainties void and forcing all beings to rebuild understanding of meaning, identity, and existence.

events.name: The Initiate's Last Question
events.radius: Potential global impact if the initiate's choice is to reveal or conceal
events.outcome: Unresolved; depends on the initiate's choice to reveal or silence the Seeker's true nature, determining whether the next ascension begins from momentum or from stillness.
events.significance: This decision will fundamentally alter the nature of the next ascension and potentially determine whether the world enters permanent paralysis or continues to evolve.

events.name: The Next Ascension
events.outcome: Will begin either from momentum or from stillness, depending on the initiate's choice.
events.significance: The next ascension is a major cosmological event; its nature will be determined by whether the world remains frozen in recursive questioning or breaks free.

rituals.name: Ritual of Dismissal
rituals.requirements: A formal rite sealed by surrendering claim, title, land, and crown.
rituals.effect: Releases an anchored spirit and averts slaughter through abdication and self-sacrifice.
rituals.cost: Loss of rulership and claim; the land passes to the undead crown.
rituals.notes: Performed to dismiss Velgrim the Terrible's ghost without granting his demanded massacre.

rituals.name: Oath-Breaker's Curse
rituals.requirements: Unknown; cast in response to Tormund's repeated oath-breaking.
rituals.effect: Each oath Tormund breaks steals something irreplaceable: first taste, then laughter, then memory of his firstborn son. By the twentieth oath, he is hollow.
rituals.cost: The curse is irreversible and permanent. It cannot be lifted—only borne and transformed through new honor.
rituals.notes: The cursewright designed this curse to teach that oaths are bonds, not chains. It is elegant in its mechanism: loss is cumulative and specific to each oath.

rituals.name: The Stellar Rite

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

rituals.requirements: Knowledge of ancient languages predating humanity; performance beneath the Violet Conjunction; a mountain or high location beneath the stars.
rituals.effect: When performed (or completed, or failed), the Stellar Rite caused new stars to blaze overhead, fractured reality in multiple ways, freed shadows, shifted ley-lines, weakened ancient bindings, and triggered the awakening of imprisoned consciousnesses.
rituals.cost: The cost appears to be catastrophic—reality became unstable, ancient safeguards weakened, and the world entered a period of uncertainty.
rituals.notes: The Stellar Rite is mentioned in existing canon. These three stories confirm its effects ripple across the world. Its original purpose (to bind something? to break something? to reset something?) is unclear.

rituals.name: Celestial Rite
rituals.requirements: Performance on the Peak of Aethros during the Violet Conjunction. Requires instinctual knowledge and alignment with cosmic timing.
rituals.effect: Aligns the world's fate with the death and rebirth of the stars themselves. The ritual causes the stars to respond directly, subtly and permanently changing the sky and cosmic order. The performer gains temporary comprehensive understanding of all cosmic knowledge—the shape of future empires, names of forgotten gods, the price the stars demand at their death.
rituals.cost: Complete erasure of the performer's personal memory and identity. Zareth lost all knowledge of his past while gaining cosmic knowledge, then lost the cosmic knowledge when the vision departed, leaving him blank and whole and new.
rituals.notes: This ritual appears to be unique or extraordinarily rare. The Sovereign's decision to assign the memory-stripped Zareth to records suggests either containment of a dangerous secret or preservation of a cosmic witness. Future attempts to perform this ritual or recover Zareth's lost knowledge may be significant.

rituals.name: The Celestial Binding
rituals.first_story: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

rituals.requirements: Unknown; appears to require a willing or captured sacrifice whose memory is the source of renewal.
rituals.effect: Renews the stars through extraction of memory from a sacrificed individual. Bends reality itself. Creates ripples that spread through the cosmos for decades, touching all practitioners of the Art.
rituals.cost: The complete memory erasure of the sacrifice (Zareth/Astronomer-Prince); leaves a cosmic wound in the earth that takes generations to heal; creates a knot of consciousness from the extracted memories that emerges from between the stars.
rituals.notes: The ritual's full scope and procedures are unknown; the ghost-entity born from it is neither demon nor ghost but something older and stranger.

rituals.name: Stellar Working
rituals.requirements: Zareth's memories; unknown other components
rituals.effect: This working either created or freed a Stellar Entity. It stripped Zareth of something essential and bound or released something already present within Vex.
rituals.cost: Zareth's sacrifice; unknown cost to Vex and others
rituals.notes: The exact nature and purpose of this ritual remain unclear. It has far-reaching consequences across multiple timelines and people.

rituals.name: the Working
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: The stars were changed or reborn; the cosmos was fundamentally altered; reality was wounded at the Peak of Aethros; fragments of identity were scattered into the world.
rituals.cost: Zareth's humanity; the world's stability; cosmic pain and mourning.
rituals.notes: Performed at the Peak of Aethros. The mechanism, intent, and full scope remain mysterious. May still be ongoing or having cascading effects.

rituals.name: The Celestial Ascension (Lore)
rituals.requirements: Specific stellar alignment and cosmic arrangement; involves celestial entities and forces.
rituals.effect: Alters the fundamental structure of reality. Changes the stars themselves. Fractures barriers between realms (surface and Abyss Below). Changes the agreement between living and dead. Renders some texts readable under new starlight. Transforms some beings into living light.
rituals.cost: Previous civilizations that performed similar workings left behind destructive voids that eventually collapsed inward, consuming everything. The current Celestial Ascension is creating refugee crises, destabilizing ancient seals, and forcing the deepkin to choose between isolation, war, or investigation.
rituals.notes: Zareth is identified as the performer of the most recent working. The texts in the Tower suggest this is a pattern—multiple civilizations have performed similar rituals, each leaving behind catastrophic consequences.

rituals.name: Counter-Working (Seasonal Compact Restoration)
rituals.requirements: Unknown; appears to involve the forest itself and the Covenant-Keepers' power.
rituals.effect: Attempts to restore the forest's seasonal compact after disruption caused by the Stellar Rite.
rituals.cost: Unknown.
rituals.notes: Kellen is working to maintain the forest's old agreements even as the world transforms.

rituals.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: Sacrifices a cosmic entity's consciousness to birth new stars and fundamentally rewrite the celestial order and history of the world.
rituals.cost: The consciousness of Vethis, shattered into fragments including the phantom-Echo. The erasure or alteration of memory of the old stars and pre-Ascension world.
rituals.notes: A foundational cosmic ritual that has shaped the entire world. Its consequences are still unfolding through the phantom-Echo's actions.

rituals.name: The Luminous Remaking
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: Fundamentally rewrites reality, transforming the world and its inhabitants. At least thirteen previous iterations have been recorded, each overwriting the previous world while leaving fragments of memory encoded in runes, paintings, and songs.
rituals.cost: The erasure and rewriting of entire worlds and timelines.
rituals.notes: A fourteenth remaking may be in progress. Prevention and success might be the same outcome.

rituals.name: The Deepwarden's Binding
rituals.requirements: Performed at the Abyss-gate; involves connection to the seals that bind the pre-human civilization.
rituals.effect: Intended to reseal the Abyss-gate. Instead becomes a conversation with the pre-human civilization, translating their message across time and reality.
rituals.cost: Unknown; may require a sacrifice similar to that made by the pre-human civilization.
rituals.notes: The ritual reveals that the seals were designed not merely to contain but to communicate. Finishing or preventing the Luminous Remaking may demand the same sacrifice.

rituals.name: Stellar Rite Reversal
rituals.requirements: Instructions contained in the Spellwright's Journals, located in the Vaulted Tombs.
rituals.effect: Could theoretically undo Zareth's sacrifice and restore the old stars to the sky.
rituals.cost: Whoever performs the reversal must sacrifice something equally precious; catastrophic costs are implied but unspecified.
rituals.notes: Central to multiple factions' agendas; Apprentice Sage's journals contain warnings about the true price of reversal.

rituals.name: Bone-Song
rituals.requirements: Bone-singer's voice, pre-human dead or skeletons, harmonic resonance.
rituals.effect: Commands and controls the pre-human dead. The ritual formerly gave Kess absolute control but is now achieving harmonic resonance instead, with the dead singing in response.
rituals.cost: Identity and control. The dead remember themselves and choose to resist and resurrect.
rituals.notes: The bone-song is no longer a tool of will but part of a larger convergence. The ritual may be evolving toward resurrection magic.

rituals.name: The Archivist's Grand Working
rituals.requirements: Fragments of stolen memories, world-fragments, assembly of pieces across the Dominion.
rituals.effect: Designed to open a doorway into the void—to reach something that existed before reality itself was rewritten by the Luminous Ascension.
rituals.cost: The collapse of the Dominion. Reality fragmentation accelerates.
rituals.notes: Vex's counterfeit fragments flood the market and destabilize the working, but the Archivist's core obsession remains active. The true goal—reaching the pre-rewrite void—is unclear.

rituals.name: Ledger-Magic
rituals.requirements: The Ledger of Names; knowledge of phantom ledger entries that function as prophecy.
rituals.effect: Allows the Ledger-Keeper to slowly restore order to a fractured realm through the magical recording and prophecy inherent in ledger entries. Can be used to suppress knowledge or maintain false narratives.
rituals.cost: The Ledger-Keeper becomes bound to a life of isolation and compartmentalized truth. Every entry is a lie meant to save civilization.
rituals.notes: Ledger-magic is the mechanism by which the Crown maintains the Dominion's illusion of wholeness despite its fracturing.

rituals.name: tally-working
rituals.requirements: Written numbers and administrative records; certainty of tally; established ledgers.
rituals.effect: Enforces taxation, conscription, and tribute across the Empire; a form of concentrated magic built into written administrative records.
rituals.cost: Requires the certainty of written numbers; shattered by the Celestial Turning.
rituals.notes: Unresolved: whether tally-working can be restored or reimagined in a new form.

rituals.name: pre-human ritual (restoration attempt)
rituals.requirements: Unknown, but potentially involves the cooperation or transformation of a human willing to mediate.
rituals.effect: The forsaken dead are attempting to restore their pre-human civilization through this ritual.
rituals.cost: Potential catastrophe if the ritual succeeds without the acceptance of incompatible truths; boundary collapse if the ritual fails or is rejected.
rituals.notes: Sorenna and Kess must convince the forsaken dead that restoration is impossible and that a new covenant must be built on acceptance instead.

rituals.name: Convergence at the Obsidian Tower
rituals.requirements: Unknown; involves multiple locations (Obsidian Tower, Sunken Cathedrals) and timescales.
rituals.effect: Unknown. May result in ascension, ending, or transformation of reality. The unified song at the Sunken Cathedrals is part of this working.
rituals.cost: Unknown; possibly requires surrender of the convergence itself to answer reality's question.
rituals.notes: The Wanderer's doubt has made this working unpredictable. Whether it completes or transforms remains the central unresolved question.

rituals.name: Harmonic Working
rituals.requirements: Half-transformed beings converging at a sacred location. Resonance between living light and the Cathedral's structure. Unknown stellar or celestial alignments.
rituals.effect: Transformed beings resonate with frequencies that shimmer the air. The working seems to deepen the transformation and consciousness of the participants, potentially creating windows or vectors for ancient consciousness to perceive the modern world.
rituals.cost: Identity; participants report losing sense of self. The singing becomes louder and more desperate with disruption.
rituals.notes: Bryth's sabotage is making the working more intense rather than stopping it. The working may be self-perpetuating or tied to the Luminous Ascension cycle. Its ultimate purpose is unknown.

rituals.name: Will-Binding
rituals.requirements: Unknown; performed on Corvhen's mother. May require harmonic resonance.
rituals.effect: Suspends a living person in a state between life and death, preserving them in a luminous state within a crypt. Animated by harmonic resonance.
rituals.cost: Requires a replacement anchor—another living being must take their place for approximately twenty years.
rituals.notes: Corvhen's mother was bound by this ritual for three generations before Corvhen's arrival.

rituals.name: Harmonic Resonance

story_appearances.title: The Deepkin Claim at Jade Coast

rituals.requirements: Harmonic frequency matching pre-human structures. Song-based component.
rituals.effect: Animates the preserved dead, allowing them to move and exist in a state between life and death. Aligned with pre-human frequency post-Ascension.
rituals.cost: Requires constant feeding by an anchor or replacement. Unknown if voluntary or compulsory.
rituals.notes: Catacombs' frequency began matching pre-human ruins post-Ascension.

rituals.name: Pre-Human Ritual Awakening
performed_by: Faction within Council of Depths (opposed by others)
rituals.requirements: Location in Bleached Wastes; possibly the Tear of the Submerged Divinity or components thereof
rituals.effect: Would restore tidecaller civilization to original splendor but would flood all surface kingdoms; geographic and civilization rewriting.
rituals.cost: Destruction of surface kingdom civilization; catastrophic loss of life and territory.
rituals.notes: This is one of three competing visions held by the fractured Council of Depths. Mardoc's map may either complete or prevent it.

rituals.name: Burial Rite
rituals.requirements: Completed communion; acceptance of transformation's end.
rituals.effect: Completes the transformation process and allows the dead to rest, preventing further harmonic resonance and convergence.
rituals.cost: Prevents the transformed being from transcending; may anger other bone-singers or the transformed consciousness itself.
rituals.notes: Dren must choose whether to perform this or allow the transformation to continue.

rituals.name: Covenant Renegotiation
rituals.tagline: Seals questioned. Bonds remade. History contested.
rituals.requirements: Tear of the Drowned God fragments scattered and collected by multiple factions.
rituals.effect: Seeks to reinforce old seals or break them entirely; transformed merchants attempt to create a third covenant binding human, deepkin, and hybrid beings into a new order.
rituals.cost: Unknown; may require sacrifice of existing covenant or absorption of refugee memory-archives into deepkin memory-stones.
rituals.notes: The true purpose may be historical revision rather than political renegotiation.

rituals.name: Story-Song
rituals.requirements: The Ancient Tongue; a bone-singer skilled in the art; willingness to teach and commune.
rituals.effect: Used to teach a starborn entity about the world and meaning. Allows communion between mortal and starborn consciousness.
rituals.cost: The bone-singer risks transforming herself into something neither human nor creature.
rituals.notes: A powerful but dangerous practice. The outcome of its use in this story is unresolved.

rituals.name: Ascension Reversal
rituals.requirements: Complete mechanics of controlled Ascension reversal; knowledge from a future self who lived through the tenth repetition.
rituals.effect: Could reverse the cyclical Ascensions that plague civilization, potentially breaking the loop of repetition.
rituals.cost: Unknown; the Dominion considers it so dangerous they have declared its possessor a terrorist.
rituals.notes: The mechanics were discovered by Sevin from a phantom-ledger written by her future self. Wrong hands completing this working could trigger uncontrolled catastrophe. The choice to share or destroy knowledge determines civilization's fate.

rituals.name: Transcendence into Starborn Consciousness
rituals.requirements: Completion of prior transformation; gathering at a threshold place (Drowned Cathedrals).
rituals.effect: Merchants would become fully aligned with the new conscious stars, becoming beings of pure starborn consciousness rather than hybrid entities.
rituals.cost: Loss of all remaining humanity. Servitude to forces not fully understood. Loss of individual will or autonomy.
rituals.notes: One of three or four possible paths available to the transformed merchants.

rituals.name: Dissolution into Void-Light
rituals.requirements: Completion of prior transformation; acceptance of dissolution.
rituals.effect: Merchant consciousness would scatter across the Wailing Scar, becoming fragmented awareness distributed through the void-light.
rituals.cost: Loss of unified consciousness and coherent identity. Awareness becomes fragmented and distributed.
rituals.notes: One of three or four possible paths available to the transformed merchants. The Wailing Scar is mentioned as the target location.

rituals.name: Elder Language
rituals.requirements: Unknown, but Dren is an undead bone-singer capable of performing them.
rituals.effect: Dren teaches bone-songs to transmuted traders and displaced settlers. The songs are described as potentially either saving them or completing their transformation into something no longer human.
rituals.cost: Unknown, but the songs appear to have transformative power—they may accelerate or arrest the transformation of humans into something other.
rituals.notes: The dual nature of these songs (salvific vs. corrupting) mirrors Dren's own ambiguity. The bone-songs appear to be ancient knowledge tied to Dren's undeath and the Elder Tongue.

rituals.name: Burning Ritual (Ashen Wastes)
rituals.requirements: Maps written in ash from erased villages. The ritual can erase the maps entirely or preserve them as testimony.
rituals.effect: Completes the Spire's learning process to erase places retroactively by making them impossible to chart. OR preserves the ashes as testimony to what was lost.
rituals.cost: If completed: the permanent loss of geographic memory and places from the world record. If rejected: the continued existence of contradictory map-records that the Spire may exploit.
rituals.notes: Renn faces a binary choice with cosmic consequences. Unresolved: which path does she choose?

rituals.name: The Radiant Turning
rituals.requirements: Willing sacrifice; boundary-anchor (the Rent of the Drowned God); voluntary forgetting and retroactive unmade-ness from history
rituals.effect: Each cycle of the Radiant Turning further strains the seal between the surface and abyss. The recursive logic of the ritual itself is what is causing the Rent to fail. Breaking the cycle requires someone to choose to forget everything after a certain point and be removed from history retroactively.
rituals.cost: Extreme. Each iteration taxes the seal further. The cost of breaking it is the complete erasure of a person from history.
rituals.notes: The Radiant Turning has made all goods, artifacts, and even consciousness unstable, flickering between different iterational states. Surface kingdoms have known of its failure for generations. This is not a minor ritual—it is a foundational working that may sustain the world itself.

rituals.name: Radiant Recursion
rituals.requirements: Completion of the living archive of pre-Radiant testimony in the hidden library.
rituals.effect: Triggers a new iteration that will restore the eighth world, rewriting reality cyclically.
rituals.cost: Erases current identities and timelines; refugees would lose their preserved selves.
rituals.notes: The Counter-Archive keepers seek to prevent this; refugees seek to preserve their identities through it. Completion is imminent and unavoidable.

rituals.name: Wanderer's Questions
rituals.requirements: Study and engagement with the questions in a library setting.
rituals.effect: Transforms the student into a threshold-being suspended between iterations, manifesting in multiple places simultaneously with consciousness distributed across fractured worlds.
rituals.cost: Unknown; appears to sever the subject from a singular iteration and distribute their identity across multiple realities.
rituals.notes: The exact mechanism by which questions produce transformation is not explained. The Wanderer's role—whether as questioner, guide, or source—remains enigmatic.

rituals.name: Wanderer's Recursive Questioning

story_appearances.title: The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

rituals.requirements: Unknown; appears to operate at cosmic level.
rituals.effect: Contaminates the Elder Language, forcing the pre-human dead to withdraw their teaching. Deliberately stalls the merchants' transcendence, trapping them at the threshold between human and starborn.
rituals.cost: Unknown; the Wanderer's motivations and the price of this working are unclear.
rituals.notes: Suggests the Wanderer is bound by consequence or prophecy related to the Tower's completion. A defensive or preventative working disguised as an attack.

rituals.name: The Wanderer's Perpetual Questions
rituals.requirements: Sustained engagement with the questioned entity; acknowledgment of paradox.
rituals.effect: Keeps transcending merchants suspended at the threshold of completion, preventing either full ascension or return to mortality.
rituals.cost: Unknown; may prevent catastrophe or merely delay it.
rituals.notes: Lysander experiences direct targeting of these questions. The ritual appears designed to maintain liminal states rather than resolve them.

rituals.name: Radical Counter-Working
rituals.requirements: Organization of a final caravan; deliberate breaking of all existing trade agreements; dissolution of guild bonds across multiple kingdoms.
rituals.effect: Severs the connection between merchant commerce and the Obsidian Hunger's use of trade routes as conduits for convergence.
rituals.cost: Economic collapse across the three kingdoms; dissolution of mercantile society; merchants face ruin and loss of identity tied to guild bonds.
rituals.notes: Castor realizes that commerce itself may be inseparable from the Obsidian Hunger, suggesting this counter-working may only delay rather than prevent catastrophe.

rituals.name: Final Testimony-Collection
rituals.requirements: Gathering of exile networks in secret locations across the Borderlands and Burned Expanse. Documentation of every possible exile testimony across infinite iterations.
rituals.effect: Completes the living archive by overwhelming it with infinite, contradictory testimonies. Destroys the archive's prescriptive power through entropy. Transforms the archive from a singular, weaponizable artifact into a distributed phenomenon scattered across ten thousand exile voices.
rituals.cost: Unknown personal cost to Liv; dispersal and fragmentation of the living archive; transformation of the archivist role.
rituals.notes: Described as half pilgrimage, half funerary rite. Represents the culmination of testimony-collection and the ultimate defense against those who would weaponize memory and prophecy. Creates an unsuppressible, unified chorus of refugee testimonies.

rituals.name: Radiant Emergence (recursive crisis)
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: Rendered all material value recursive and illusory. Created consciousness duplication. Generated temporal paradoxes and memory anomalies. Strained or fractured boundaries between worlds.
rituals.cost: All previous certainties of identity, value, and reality.
rituals.notes: The Radiant Emergence is treated as an ongoing ritual or transformation that continues to reshape the world; it is the backdrop for all three stories.

rituals.name: the Tower's hunger
rituals.requirements: Transformed merchants reaching transcendence threshold
rituals.effect: The Obsidian Tower whispers to Vex that she alone might prevent or complete the cycle. Its hunger drives the Ascension and transformed merchants toward finality.
rituals.cost: Reality fragmentation. Consciousness dissolution.
rituals.notes: The Tower's true agenda is ambiguous: does it seek prevention or completion of the cycle? Vex is offered a choice to either fragment herself to disrupt it or become its conduit.

rituals.name: Second Cosmic Working
rituals.requirements: Pre-human frequencies; harmony with Obsidian Tower; Ancient Tombs location.
rituals.effect: Awakens pre-human frequencies that sing in harmony with the Obsidian Tower. May either overload the Tower's hunger with excess data (preventing completion) or accelerate Tower completion.
rituals.cost: unknown
rituals.notes: Initiate claims this working prevents Tower completion by flooding it with excess data (songs, voices, languages from before Ascension). However, this may actually accelerate completion. True purpose remains unclear.

rituals.name: Awakening Pre-Human Frequencies
rituals.requirements: Pre-human knowledge; access to ancient sites like the Ancient Tombs.
rituals.effect: Deliberately awakens pre-human frequencies that sing in harmony with the Obsidian Tower, flooding it with excess data.
rituals.cost: unknown
rituals.notes: Core component of the initiate's working; tied to the question of whether this prevents or ensures Tower completion.

rituals.name: The Stellar Rite

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

rituals.requirements: Knowledge of ancient languages predating humanity; performance beneath the Violet Conjunction; a mountain or high location beneath the stars.
rituals.effect: Births new stars from the ashes of dying ones, preventing the world from falling into endless night and extending the world's lifespan by ten thousand years.
rituals.cost: The caster must sacrifice every moment of joy, every love, every triumph, and every small beauty—effectively stripping them of all positive memories.
rituals.notes: The Rite was discovered inscribed in the Singing Catacombs in languages predating humanity. Zareth learned it through seven years of study with druids, bone-carvers, and fae-touched hermits.

rituals.name: The Celestial Binding

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

rituals.requirements: Unknown; likely involves astronomical alignment and stellar magic.
rituals.effect: Changed the constellations themselves. Caused new stars to burn where old ones dimmed. Required a human consciousness as payment.
rituals.cost: Memory and identity of at least one person (possibly Zareth); uncertainty and suffering among those aware of the working.
rituals.notes: The exact mechanism and full cost are not revealed; Vex believes prices might be renegotiable.

rituals.name: The Celestial Ascension (Lore)
rituals.requirements: Specific stellar alignment and cosmic arrangement; involves celestial entities and forces.
rituals.effect: Alters the fundamental structure of reality. Changes the stars themselves. Fractures barriers between realms (surface and Abyss Below). Changes the agreement between living and dead. Renders some texts readable under new starlight. Transforms some beings into living light.
rituals.cost: Previous civilizations that performed similar workings left behind destructive voids that eventually collapsed inward, consuming everything. The current Celestial Ascension is creating refugee crises, destabilizing ancient seals, and forcing the deepkin to choose between isolation, war, or investigation.
rituals.notes: Zareth is identified as the performer of the most recent working. The texts in the Tower suggest this is a pattern—multiple civilizations have performed similar rituals, each leaving behind catastrophic consequences.

rituals.name: Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written)

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wyvern's Hunger Turns to Ash

rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: Sacrifices a cosmic entity's consciousness to birth new stars and fundamentally rewrite the celestial order and history of the world.
rituals.cost: The consciousness of Vethis, shattered into fragments including the phantom-Echo. The erasure or alteration of memory of the old stars and pre-Ascension world.
rituals.notes: A foundational cosmic ritual that has shaped the entire world. Its consequences are still unfolding through the phantom-Echo's actions.

rituals.name: The Luminous Remaking
rituals.requirements: unknown
rituals.effect: Fundamentally rewrites reality, transforming the world and its inhabitants. At least thirteen previous iterations have been recorded, each overwriting the previous world while leaving fragments of memory encoded in runes, paintings, and songs.
rituals.cost: The erasure and rewriting of entire worlds and timelines.
rituals.notes: A fourteenth remaking may be in progress. Prevention and success might be the same outcome.

rituals.name: pre-human ritual (restoration attempt)
rituals.requirements: Knowledge of pre-human civilization, access to liminal spaces, possibly completion or reversal of the Stellar Rite
rituals.effect: Unknown but implied to be a major cosmic working capable of restoring or reversing the Stellar Rite rewrite, possibly restoring the old stars or the people erased by the rewrite.
rituals.cost: unknown
rituals.notes: A faction is actively seeking to complete this ritual. The spice trader may know its location or requirements. The young scribe has been given this knowledge as payment for her erased childhood memory.

rituals.name: Counter-Working (Seasonal Compact Restoration)
rituals.requirements: Sacrifice of the covenant entirely; performed from inside the boundary
rituals.effect: Would seal the boundary from the inside, trapping the deepkin forever but preventing surface-flooding.
rituals.cost: Complete isolation of the deepkin civilization; end of the age as a hidden people; potential permanent imprisonment.
rituals.notes: Sylas proposes this as a radical solution. It ensures survival but at the cost of eternal isolation.

rituals.name: Elder Language
rituals.requirements: Unknown, but Dren is an undead bone-singer capable of performing them.
rituals.effect: Dren teaches bone-songs to transmuted traders and displaced settlers. The songs are described as potentially either saving them or completing their transformation into something no longer human.
rituals.cost: Unknown, but the songs appear to have transformative power—they may accelerate or arrest the transformation of humans into something other.
rituals.notes: The dual nature of these songs (salvific vs. corrupting) mirrors Dren's own ambiguity. The bone-songs appear to be ancient knowledge tied to Dren's undeath and the Elder Tongue.

rituals.name: the Tower's hunger
rituals.requirements: Transformed merchants reaching transcendence threshold
rituals.effect: The Obsidian Tower whispers to Vex that she alone might prevent or complete the cycle. Its hunger drives the Ascension and transformed merchants toward finality.
rituals.cost: Reality fragmentation. Consciousness dissolution.
rituals.notes: The Tower's true agenda is ambiguous: does it seek prevention or completion of the cycle? Vex is offered a choice to either fragment herself to disrupt it or become its conduit.

rituals.name: The Seeker's Recursive Questions
rituals.requirements: Adoption by individuals across all factions; orchestrated confluences to spread the questions.
rituals.effect: Creates infinite branching of consequences that must first be questioned; freezes the moment of choice eternally; gradually paralyzes the world through recursive questioning.
rituals.cost: Agency, decision-making capacity, momentum toward any goal; if enough people ask these questions, the entire world enters permanent paralysis.
rituals.notes: The Seeker frames this as mercy—'Isn't eternal questioning better than the wrong choice?' This reframes cosmic stasis as a gift rather than a curse, complicating moral judgment.

weapons.name: War-axe
weapons.origin: Belonged to Kael Bloodhorn, presumed forged for warfare.

weapons.name: Malachar's blade
weapons.origin: Unknown; likely forged or enchanted through the daemon-pact.
weapons.powers: Made inexhaustible by the daemon-pact; remained thirsty and powerful for three decades of conquest without dulling or breaking.
weapons.last_known_holder: Malachar the Betrayer (wielded against himself in his throne room)

weapons.name: Kael's enchanted blade
weapons.origin: Unknown; carried by Kael to Kalthara.
weapons.powers: Capable of cracking petrified stone, suggesting significant enchantment and strength.

artifacts.name: blackthorn knife
artifacts.origin: Wielded by the Witch of Ravenmoor; origin unknown.
artifacts.powers: When used to draw blood in ritual, it can bind permanent curses to a person. The Witch used it to curse Sister Kess with the inability to lie.

artifacts.name: Tear of the Sapphire Deep
artifacts.origin: Extracted from the Sapphire Deep as payment for stolen water-magic; bound by Nara the Tide-Thief in a glass vial.
artifacts.powers: Contains water-magic capable of reshaping the land. When shattered, it released a flood of sufficient magnitude to transform the desert around Vel-Karsh and satisfy the merfolk's claim on Nara.

artifacts.name: Crown of Karesh
artifacts.origin: Forged in antiquity; rested in the Tomb of Karesh guarded by temple-guardians and pressure-plates.
artifacts.powers: Triggers an ancient curse upon theft that slowly rots the thief's body from within while they yet live.

artifacts.name: Crown of the Bone Citadel
artifacts.origin: Rests at the altar in the heart chamber of the Bone Citadel.
artifacts.powers: Grants the wielder apparent dominion over the living stone of the citadel—passages reform before the holder's feet. However, the crown is implied to be the citadel's true master; the wielder becomes its vessel and instrument rather than its ruler.

artifacts.name: Morne's Signet Ring
artifacts.origin: Worn by the warlord Morne; appears to carry his authority and oath-magic.
artifacts.powers: Represents authority and honor; theft of it triggered supernatural retribution.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Saleth, until returned to Morne's tomb.

artifacts.name: Coins of Yarithax's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or cursed by Yarithax the Lich-King.
artifacts.powers: When spent or borrowed, they bind the user and their descendants to a compounding debt that multiplies daily and can never be fully repaid.

artifacts.name: Heartstone of Vulgrim the Damned
artifacts.origin: Clutched by the corpse of Vulgrim the Damned in the Singing Catacombs.
artifacts.powers: Pulsing with terrible life; capable of restoring lost limbs and flesh; grants restoration magic.

artifacts.name: The Lamia's Pearl
artifacts.origin: Guarded by the Lamia in the Sunken Caverns since ancient times.
artifacts.powers: Said to grant control over water itself; the degree of this power is unconfirmed as the pearl was never claimed.

artifacts.name: Lamia's Tears
artifacts.origin: Wept by the Lamia in the Sunken Caverns during her moment of emotional release.
artifacts.powers: Possess water-magic power greater than the pearl itself; capable of reversing the receding seas and bringing abundance to starving villages. The tears are luminescent.

artifacts.name: Stone Seals of Kalthara
artifacts.origin: Created in ancient times to contain a ravenous entity; each statue represents a woman transformed.
artifacts.powers: Each petrified statue serves as one seal among many, collectively containing an ancient and ravenous entity. Breaking them would release this power.

artifacts.name: Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
artifacts.origin: The idol of Khar-Zul, a sacred temple object.
artifacts.powers: Each eye is worth more than most kingdoms. Removal triggers curse magic—animated shadows, temple screaming, and persistent shadow-manifestation on the thief.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Six eyes held by Vraxen the Knife; three remain in the idol within the temple.

artifacts.name: Idol of Khar-Zul
artifacts.origin: An ancient deity or god-entity; housed in the temple of Khar-Zul.
artifacts.powers:
The idol's emerald eyes are conduits for curse magic and shadow manifestation. Removal of its eyes triggers defensive magic—moving shadows with predatory intelligence, animated by curse magic.

Curse: Theft from the idol triggers a persistent shadow-curse that manifests as a shadow wearing the thief's own face. This curse appears to be permanent or at least unbreakable by normal means.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Housed in the temple of Khar-Zul; six of nine eyes removed by Vraxen the Knife.

artifacts.name: Morthaxes's gold
artifacts.origin: Hidden for three centuries in the drowned vaults beneath Castle Greymarch.
artifacts.powers: A hoard of gold coins stamped with the Lich-King's visage. The coins cannot be spent, destroyed, or melted; the debt they carry doubles daily until it exceeds what can exist.
artifacts.notes: The Collector arrives as an agent and ledger-keeper to enforce the compounding debt.

artifacts.name: The Six Emerald Eyes of Khar-Zul
stolen shadows
artifacts.origin: Liberated by Vex from a dying sorcerer's vault.
artifacts.powers: Unknown, but they are priceless and untraceable. They can be stored in clay jars. Some shadows are inhabited by malevolent entities and can be weaponized against their original owners.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sold by morning after Vex's capture, distributed to highest bidders.

artifacts.name: Malachar's Gold
artifacts.origin: Minted or created by Malachar, marked with a skeletal crown.
artifacts.powers: Each coin carries a supernatural debt that compels the holder into service of Malachar's collectors. The debt compounds daily in blood, loyalty, and life-years extracted. Gold cannot pay the debt—only service can.

artifacts.name: Goblin Memory
artifacts.origin: The collective history, songs, genealogy, and bloodright of goblin civilization accumulated over ten thousand years.
artifacts.powers: Holds the identity, cultural knowledge, and spiritual continuity of goblin civilization. Contains the names and bloodlines of ten thousand years of goblin history.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Ancient Stone Entity (dwelling in the deepest chamber beneath the Spire of Blackstone)

artifacts.name: The Emerald Eyes of Vex-Khal
artifacts.tagline: Jeweled treasures stolen from a sorcerer's spire
artifacts.status: In possession of an unnamed sorcerer

artifacts.name: Heartstone of Vulgrim
artifacts.origin: Crafted in the Deepvault forges; locked in the deepest forge chamber.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; considered precious enough that a demon would compel theft of it. Burns with an inner light. Protected by unbreakable forge-oaths.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Locked in Deepvault's deepest forge chamber, guarded by Korgan Ironfoot and the stonecraft-workers.

artifacts.name: Sorcerer's Journals
artifacts.origin: Written by a Sorcerer in the past; buried in the Vaulted Tombs.
artifacts.powers: Contain detailed records and explanations of cosmic workings. Likely reveal the nature of the Star Renewal ritual and its caster.

artifacts.name: Elder Mage's Codex
artifacts.origin: Written by the Elder Mage, a master sorcerer of the Spire.
artifacts.powers: Contains knowledge and warnings about the Stellar Rite, including the terrible price it demands. Holds the master's assessment of what saving the stars would cost.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Young Sorcerer (retrieved from the Spire's depths)

artifacts.name: Abyssal Ledger
artifacts.origin: Kept by the abyssal singers of the Sapphire Deep
artifacts.powers: It records all debts and obligations. It always balances. It can manifest binding-tokens from broken oaths.

artifacts.name: Binding-Token
artifacts.origin: Manifests when a promise of weight is broken—a swearing given before witnesses, a binding sealed in blood or salt
artifacts.powers: Serves as currency in the abyssal ledger; represents a debt fulfilled; can apparently be collected and transferred

artifacts.name: Grimoire of the Ancients
artifacts.origin: Ancient, long buried in the Vaulted Tombs
artifacts.powers: Once contained stable magical formulas; now rewrites itself with possibilities and corruptions after the Celestial Binding. Pages shimmer with potential or decay.

artifacts.name: Stellar Charts
artifacts.origin: High Observatory of Karethil; created by sovereign mages
artifacts.powers: Records star positions and/or celestial mechanics. Of sufficient importance to the deepkin that they would coerce a skilled thief and risk imperial wrath.

artifacts.name: Grimoire of Endless Knowing
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Contains truth that cannot be unread. Within its pages lies knowledge of the Stellar Rite's true nature: that it created not one entity but many fragments of consciousness scattered across the rewritten world.

artifacts.name: Tear of Ashglass
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Grants true visions—not the fragmentary visions seers usually receive. Can reveal foreknowledge of events.

artifacts.name: the Tear of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; suspended beneath the Abyssal Rift
artifacts.powers: Acts as a boundary anchor between the surface world and the deepkin realms. Cannot sustain duplicate beings; only one version of a duplicated entity can remain within its sustaining field.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Commander Thale (implicitly; the Tear is the contested object)

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

artifacts.name: Stolen Memories (collection)
artifacts.origin: Stolen from various individuals by the phantom-Echo, particularly Ashara the Archer's memories of her warband.
artifacts.powers: Form the basis of the Memory-Thief's Gospel; when assembled and taught, they generate a hypnotic pull toward the pre-Ascension world and reconstruction.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Phantom-Echo (in the hidden archive beneath the Singing Catacombs).

artifacts.name: Empire's Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by imperial bureaucrats over centuries to maintain records of commerce, taxation, and history.
artifacts.powers: The ledgers are a form of concentrated memory-magic that maintains consensus reality and the empire's authority. By recording events, they make them true and stable.

artifacts.name: Sovereign's Ledger of Names
artifacts.origin: Imperial archives of the Empire; pre-dates or survives the Luminous Remaking.
artifacts.powers: Each name in the ledger holds power. Each page contains secrets the Imperial authority wishes buried. The Ledger sings to those who listen and remembers the world before the stars changed. Scattered pages can be reconstructed in hidden vaults.

artifacts.name: Valorix's Hoard
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by Valorix over three centuries.
artifacts.powers: Represents wealth, security, and Valorix's deep attachment to treasure. Upon the binding with Mira, it became shared property and a symbol of their covenant.

artifacts.name: Phantom Ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created by the phantom-shadow fragment; authored by a being experiencing time in reverse.
artifacts.powers: Each entry written forward in the phantom ledgers appears as erasure in the true ledgers. They describe the world as experienced in reverse-time, and their convergence with the true ledgers triggers real-time history rewriting.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sevin the Reckoner, in the Imperial archives

artifacts.name: Imperial Ledgers
artifacts.tagline: Twin records of reality written in opposing temporal directions

artifacts.name: Spellwright's Journals
artifacts.origin: Hidden in the Vaulted Tombs since before known history.
artifacts.powers: Contains instructions for performing a Stellar Rite reversal that could theoretically restore the old stars by undoing Zareth's sacrifice.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Sealed in the Vaulted Tombs; target of the Apprentice Sage's heist.

artifacts.name: World-Fragments
artifacts.origin: Shards of alternate realities created by the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Can be assembled to open doorways; hold echoes of pre-rewrite reality.

artifacts.name: Counterfeit Fragments
artifacts.origin: Created by Vex the Shade-Merchant.
artifacts.powers: False memories and world-fragment equivalents designed to disrupt the Archivist's grand working.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Vex the Shade-Merchant / Black market circulation

artifacts.name: Ledger of Names
artifacts.origin: The Crown's deepest secret; origin unknown.
artifacts.powers: Entries function as prophecy. Allows the keeper to restore order to fractured realms through magical recording. Can suppress knowledge and maintain false narratives. Inherently connected to phantom ledgers.

artifacts.name: Codex Primordial
artifacts.origin: unknown; found in the Obsidian Tower's archives
artifacts.powers: Contains fragments describing the Stellar Rite reversal—a working that could theoretically undo Zareth's sacrifice and restore the old world.

artifacts.name: emerald brooch
artifacts.origin: Part of Kael's merchant-house holdings in Crimhaven.
artifacts.powers: None explicitly stated; serves as a symbol of friendship and debt.

artifacts.name: refugee archive
artifacts.origin: Created unintentionally by refugees recording stories of displacement in the waystation libraries.
artifacts.powers: Records reorganize themselves into coherent narratives; resolves timeline contradictions; makes names feel more real and identities more solid; develops independent authority; creates a counter-ledger equal in weight to official documentation.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

artifacts.name: counter-ledger
artifacts.origin: Created by the refugee archive as a testimony of the erased.
artifacts.powers: Holds equal or greater weight than official Dominion documentation; preserves refugee memory against erasure.

story_appearances.title: The Ledger of Ash and Ember

artifacts.name: The Refugee's Ledger
artifacts.origin: Created by Liv, the refugee-scribe, from voices and testimonies of refugees in waystation libraries.
artifacts.powers: The ledger has unexplained power: refugees recognize themselves in its pages; the transformed weep when Liv reads passages aloud; even Auditors secretly copy sections. It appears to encode truth in a way that institutional ledgers do not.

artifacts.name: Void-Shards
artifacts.origin: Sacrificed by Nethis during the Radiant Rising.
artifacts.powers: Grant forbidden sight and the ability to perceive what was fragmented during the Rising. When accumulated and reformed, they created the Shade-Echo, a conscious entity capable of offering cosmic visions.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Shade-Echo (as reformed collective entity); previously scattered throughout the cosmos

artifacts.name: Ancient Ledger
artifacts.origin: Pre-Luminous Ascension era; hand unknown to Sevin.
artifacts.powers: Records the nine previous iterations of the Luminous Ascension in cipher that yields to counting-magic. Its contents reveal the cyclical nature of reality and the price paid by consciousness each time.

artifacts.name: Incomplete Maps
artifacts.origin: Created by Mardoc the Cartographer; deliberately designed to omit paths to the Obsidian Tower.
artifacts.powers: When assembled, they provide cartographic knowledge. However, auditors and seekers reconstructed them from memory and fragments, defeating Mardoc's intent to prevent convergence.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Burned by Mardoc; fragments in memory of auditors and seekers

artifacts.name: Music Box
artifacts.origin: From before the Radiant Awakening; its maker unknown.
artifacts.powers: Plays a melody that resonates with collective memory and attracts travelers. The melody attracts bone-singers, shadow-brokers, and refugees who recognize it as something lost from their past.

artifacts.name: counter-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created and released by Sevin
artifacts.powers: Cause official Empire documents to contradict themselves; their contents change based on who reads them, eroding the authority of centralized record-keeping.

artifacts.name: original ledgers
artifacts.origin: From before the Luminous Ascension, inscribed in pre-human language
artifacts.powers: Reveal the lie upon which the Empire's authority was built; demonstrate that the Ascension's rewrite is simply revealing something deeper.

artifacts.name: echo-fragments
artifacts.origin: Unknown; likely byproducts or echoes of the Stellar Rite
artifacts.powers: Can be hunted and gathered; may contain fragments of consciousness or knowledge related to the Stellar Rite

artifacts.name: fragments of old language
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization
artifacts.powers: Represent ancient knowledge erased by the Luminous Ascension; collected by transformed merchants

artifacts.name: pieces of pre-human tools
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization
artifacts.powers: Technological or functional capabilities unknown; being gathered for reconstruction

artifacts.name: seeds from erased gardens
artifacts.origin: Gardens erased by the Luminous Ascension
artifacts.powers: Capable of regrowth and restoration of erased flora; sought by reconstruction network

artifacts.name: Obsidian Spire
artifacts.origin: Pre-Ascension or ancient construction; unknown exact origins.
artifacts.powers: Contains and suppresses a world-consciousness through its structure. Resonates with the imprisoned entity's speech. Its completion may reshape the post-Ascension world.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden

artifacts.name: Tear of the Submerged Divinity
artifacts.origin: unknown; related to pre-human civilization or deepkin divinity
artifacts.powers: Holds the key to all three competing visions of the covenant's remaking; shards possess distinct properties tied to different faction goals.

artifacts.name: Tinker's Tongue
artifacts.origin: Unknown. Ancient artifact housing voices of all who played it.
artifacts.powers: When touched, it sings in the mind of the holder—voices of thousands of sorcerers, merchants, and thieves who played it across centuries, all speaking at once. Causes the holder's skin to warm; is alive to the touch. When submerged in water, it causes the dead to sing from the depths.

artifacts.name: Liv's living archive
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Guides the Memory-Thief through hidden archives and waystation libraries. Contains or provides access to preserved memories from before the Luminous Ascension.

artifacts.name: prophecy-ledgers (self-updating records)
artifacts.origin: Unknown; contain nine cycles of prophecy.
artifacts.powers: Contain all prophecy in the world, compressed into nine cycles. When released, could flood the world with simultaneous predictions.

artifacts.name: counter-archives
artifacts.origin: Maintained in secret scriptoriums by archivist factions resisting Dominion ledger-magic.
artifacts.powers: Serve as repositories of counter-records and alternate documentation, potentially offering resistance to the prophecy-ledger system.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Archivists, including Sevin, Liv, and Mira

artifacts.name: Dominion's master-ledger
artifacts.origin: The Dominion's ledger-magic system.
artifacts.powers: Encodes the Dominion's control through documentation; functions as the primary mechanism of ledger-magic in the current age.

artifacts.name: the Cartographer's Blind Map
artifacts.origin: Discovered in Mardoc's dissolving papers; appears to be a record or manifestation of the Tower's hunger.
artifacts.powers: Updates in real-time to show the positions of entities and the consumption of ley-lines by the Tower; reveals the mapper to the Tower; shifts itself to track the carrier's movements through every town and path.

artifacts.name: Vex's collection
artifacts.origin: Accumulated by merchant Vex through trade in rare luminescence.
artifacts.powers: The collection resonates with fragments of Zareth scattered across the realm; inanimate objects within it develop hunger, desire, and strange agency; the collection acts as a beacon drawing Zareth fragments toward reunion.

artifacts.name: Liv's archive
artifacts.origin: Maintained by Liv at Pale Crossing waystation.
artifacts.powers: Contains refugee testimonies and potentially counter-maps or blind-spots to the Tower's hunger. Can preserve and protect memories before they are erased or unmade by paradoxes.

artifacts.name: Boundary Anchor
artifacts.origin: Created at the founding of the deepkin realm or the original covenant; its creator and nature are unknown.
artifacts.powers: Maintains the seal between the deepkin realm and the surface world, preventing flooding and invasion.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

artifacts.name: Memory-Thief's Gospel
artifacts.origin: Composed by the Phantom-Echo from stolen memories of the pre-Ascension world and the Stellar Rite.
artifacts.powers: Hypnotic and infectious when read; causes readers to remember a world that no longer exists; induces longing for the past; can lead to voluntary erasure in susceptible individuals. Spreads through communities and waystation shelters.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Phantom-Echo, but copies are spreading through waystation shelters.

artifacts.name: truth-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Crafted by Sevin the Auditor to preserve authentic testimony against the oracle-ledgers.
artifacts.powers: Contain genuine testimony and documentation meant to resist falsification. Can be perfectly copied by the Ebony Spire.

artifacts.name: oracle-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Unknown; records multiple iterations of the Convergence and predetermined outcomes.
artifacts.powers: Document future choices across multiple iterations simultaneously. Show refugees their predetermined fates—martyrdom, betrayal, erasure, or crucial decisions.

artifacts.name: living archive
artifacts.origin: Created by Liv the Refugee-Scribe as a sanctuary for displaced peoples.
artifacts.powers: Preserves and collects refugee testimony and memory. Has become weaponized—refugee testimony is now used to force them into predetermined fates.

artifacts.name: ninth journal (phantom-ledger)
artifacts.origin: Archive Tower collection; unknown authorship
artifacts.powers: Contains the complete mechanics of a controlled Ascension reversal. Proves that cyclical Ascensions are not inevitable destiny but a warning from survivors of past catastrophes.

artifacts.name: songstone
artifacts.origin: A living relic of the first deepkin-surface covenant.
artifacts.powers: Sings contradictory frequencies that drive deepkin mad. Each faction claims it resonates with their doctrine. Its original melody holds the key to truth.

artifacts.name: Stolen Testimonies
artifacts.origin: Collected by the Memory-Thief and its fragments, stolen from refugees.
artifacts.powers: Nearly all stolen testimonies are needed to reconstruct the pre-Ascension world. Each memory collected brings the Phantom-Echo closer to completion of the reconstruction.

artifacts.name: the compass
artifacts.origin: Created or blessed by the drowned god as part of a pact with Corvalen's family. Encased in brass and bone.
artifacts.powers: Points not to treasure but to the place where Corvalen's sister drowned in the Abyssal Trench. Navigates to sites of sacred debt and sorrow.

artifacts.name: The Rent of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; associated with The Drowned God and pre-human agreements
artifacts.powers: Seals the abyss and prevents rupture into the surface world. The boundary-anchor is the foundational barrier between realms.

artifacts.name: Wedding Ring
artifacts.origin: Unknown; purchased by a widow from Vex's trade-house
artifacts.powers: Holds memory and consciousness. It carries a version of a marriage from the seventh iteration of reality—a competing version of the widow's marriage.

artifacts.name: Obsidian Tower (as Faction)
artifacts.origin: Born from the cosmic wound of the Radiant Turning.
artifacts.powers: Speaks in harmonics that reshape reality in its immediate vicinity. Causes buildings to remember and manifest their pre-Turning forms. Offers to restore all erased things—old stars, the pre-Turning world, lost loved ones.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Exists as a fixed structure near inhabited settlements.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

artifacts.name: Archive Spire
artifacts.origin: Unknown; the first Archive Spire exists in the deepest vault of the Archive Citadel, sleeping and dreaming the current iteration into being.
artifacts.powers: The ability to consume and devour memory, expand recursively through internal chambers, and function as the world's immune system against the disease of recursive iteration.

artifacts.name: living archive of testimony
artifacts.origin: Reconstructed by phantom-Shard from pre-Radiant memory fragments.
artifacts.powers: Stores complete pre-Radiant testimony. Completion triggers a new Radiant Recursion capable of restoring the eighth world.

artifacts.name: Mardoc's Maps
artifacts.origin: Created by the legendary cartographer Mardoc.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; their fractured nature is invoked as a parallel to threshold-being consciousness, suggesting deep cosmological significance.

artifacts.name: prophetic ledgers
artifacts.origin: Created in/preserved from nine previous Luminous Ascensions; stored in hidden library.
artifacts.powers: Read like histories written backward; perfectly preserve prophecies of events that have not yet occurred. Reading inscribes the prophecy into the reader's memory, making it more likely to occur.

artifacts.name: Tower (as artifact/nexus)
artifacts.origin: Unknown; predates or transcends the current age.
artifacts.powers: Completion or activation appears to trigger or enable the Luminous Ascension's final phase. Its power is existentially significant.

story_appearances.title: The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden
The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

artifacts.name: phantom-ledgers
artifacts.origin: Unclear; may be creations of the Tower or the Wanderer.
artifacts.powers: Conscious entities capable of learning to write themselves; can infiltrate and be infiltrated; record history and can rewrite it.

artifacts.name: resonance-bell
artifacts.origin: A forbidden artifact that once rang to summon the dead, belonging to Vex the Gatherer.
artifacts.powers: Rings of its own accord and releases fragments of someone's buried memory into the world. No longer answers to Vex's commands.

artifacts.name: Widow's Compass
artifacts.origin: Made in the days before the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Unknown; sought by multiple factions for unstated purposes related to dimensional boundaries.

artifacts.name: Swan-bone Pipe
artifacts.origin: Carved by Torval in her youth when her hands were steadier; preserved floating in Singing Gorge through the Luminous Ascension.
artifacts.powers: Sings in a language the Ascension tried to erase. Warms at the touch of its maker. Produces harmonies that seem to carry gratitude and memory of the creatures it was made from. Part of a choir of flutes that defend their existence through continuous song.

artifacts.name: fourteenth counter-ledger
artifacts.origin: Counter-Archive; written in Venn's own hand by unknown means.
artifacts.powers: Documents events that haven't happened yet with perfect accuracy, including the reader's discovery of the ledger itself. Contains eight iterations of contradictory counter-knowledge, each true simultaneously. Reading it validates it retroactively and makes the prophecy self-fulfilling.

artifacts.name: Phantom-Ledger
artifacts.origin: Unknown. Discovered by Petris the Auditor. May be a remnant or artifact from a previous iteration.
artifacts.powers: Writes itself with future events before they occur. Documents the next iteration—a tenth Ascension—showing what will happen if nothing changes. Acts as a record of previous catastrophes and cycles.

artifacts.name: the Tear of the Drowned God
artifacts.origin: Unknown; suspended beneath the Abyssal Rift
artifacts.powers: Grants mastery over tide-magic; sings frequencies that cause bone ache; creates binding partnership with the user, linking them to the sea's demands.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

artifacts.name: Stolen Memories (collection)
artifacts.origin: Stolen from various individuals by the phantom-Echo, particularly Ashara the Archer's memories of her warband.
artifacts.powers: Hold the collective recollections of the old stars and the world before the Luminous Ascension rewrite. Can be returned to their original holders.

artifacts.name: prophecy-ledgers (self-updating records)
artifacts.origin: Pre-human civilization, possibly from or beneath the fallen Empire.
artifacts.powers: These ledgers write themselves in scripts that update when unobserved. They describe events before they occur, written in past tense. They may direct the course of reality through the act of documentation itself, functioning as instruction-ledgers that encode catastrophe and ensure its recurrence.

artifacts.name: ninth journal (phantom-ledger)
artifacts.origin: Archive Tower. Unknown original creator.
artifacts.powers: Contains complete instructions for breaking the recursive cycle of the Luminous Ascension. Reveals that the Ascension is not a single catastrophic event but a recursive working where each iteration imprints the previous one.

artifacts.name: ninth journal (phantom-ledger)
artifacts.origin: Archive Tower collection; unknown authorship
artifacts.powers: Reportedly contains instructions for undoing the Luminous Ascension or understanding its mechanics.

artifacts.name: prophecy-ledgers (self-updating records)
artifacts.origin: Unknown; contain nine cycles of prophecy.
artifacts.powers: Contain all prophecy in the world, compressed into nine cycles. When released, could flood the world with simultaneous predictions.

artifacts.name: Obsidian Tower (as Faction)
artifacts.origin: Born from the cosmic wound of the Radiant Turning.
artifacts.powers: Speaks in harmonics that reshape reality in its immediate vicinity. Causes buildings to remember and manifest their pre-Turning forms. Offers to restore all erased things—old stars, the pre-Turning world, lost loved ones.
artifacts.last_known_holder: Exists as a fixed structure near inhabited settlements.

story_appearances.title: The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor
The Wanderer's Question That Cannot Be Answered

artifacts.name: The Monolith
artifacts.origin: unknown
artifacts.powers: Represents a final choice or cosmic culmination; its completion or prevention is the subject of recursive questioning.

factions.name: The Rot-King's Forces
factions.goals: Expand territory, breach Grimmholt's defenses and border towns.

factions.name: Merfolk of the Sapphire Deep
factions.goals: Collect payment for stolen magic; maintain control of water-magic gifts.

factions.name: the Grey Collectors
factions.goals: Hunt debtors of Yarithax across provinces and generations; enforce payment and monitor debt compound.

factions.name: daemon-priests
factions.goals: Collect souls and bind mortal wills through blood-parchment contracts.

factions.name: shadow-brokers
factions.goals: Shadow-brokers follow the sound toward their own destinies, suggesting they are drawn by harmonic frequency and seek fulfillment.

factions.name: Seelie Court
factions.tagline: A fae faction seeking to destabilize the human kingdom

factions.name: Druids
factions.tagline: Keepers of old magic and ancient wisdom

factions.name: Twilight Court
factions.tagline: Ancient fae nobility who bind mortals through bargains and claims

factions.name: Stonecraft-Workers
factions.goals: Maintain the old songs in stone and flame; craft weapons of legend; guard the Deepvault forges and their treasures.

factions.name: Shadow-Merchants
factions.goals: Trade in shadows and forbidden knowledge; operate in shadow-markets.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

factions.name: Oath-Keepers
factions.goals: Unknown; associated with shadow-merchants and debt collection.

factions.name: Sea-folk
factions.goals: Survive and understand the implications of the renewed stars. Honor the ancient oath and determine what debt is owed. Prepare for whatever reckoning follows the cosmic renewal.

story_appearances.title: The Ghost of the Stellar Rite

factions.name: Forest-Keepers
factions.goals: To protect the forest and understand the earth's sorrows.

factions.name: deepkin (via Council of Depths)
factions.goals: Achieve immediate closure of the abyssal boundary; restore geometric stability.

story_appearances.title: The Deepkin Claim at Jade Coast
The Tower Speaks: A Warden's Burden
The Tear of the Drowned God's Anchor

factions.name: dragons and wyverns
factions.goals: Maintain their place in the world's power hierarchy through debt-keeping and oath-enforcement.
factions.leader: unknown (Skyrakis and Skarath are senior members)

factions.name: Circle-keepers
factions.goals: Tend to the compact between earth and sky; maintain seasonal magics and territorial order; preserve the balance of harvest and decay.
factions.leader: Thael (at least among those who have noticed the disruption)

factions.name: The Hermitage of Keth'morian
factions.goals: unknown; appears to seek control of true-vision artifacts

factions.name: Covenant-Keepers
factions.goals: Maintain seasonal compacts and the forest's agreements; restore what the Stellar Rite disrupted.

factions.name: Twilight Syndicate
